issue_title: Context Manager based notifier
issue_body:
Just a random silly idea: using a context manager to help with notifying about tasks

Would intergrate well and easily with personal python scripts n' stuff, or any codebases which would benefit from being able to easily add in notifications (buildbots perhaps?)

(note: the following code isn't exactly clean or complete, if you want this to be made into a pull request or whatever, I can)

``` python
class TaskNotifier:
    def __init__(self, task_name, on_enter=True, on_exit=True, on_error=True,
                 config=None, **kwargs):
        self.task_name = task_name
        self.on_enter = on_enter
        self.on_exit = on_exit
        self.on_error = on_error
        self.config = config
        self.kwargs = kwargs

    def __enter__(self):
        # start_time = now()
        if self.on_enter:
            # questionmark notification icon?
            ntfy.notify("Task: {}".format(self.task_name),
                        "task has started!")

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback):
        # task_time = start_time - now()
        # task_time = enprettyfy(task_time)
        if exc_type and self.on_error:
            # X notification icon?
            ntfy.notify("Task: {}".format(self.task_name),
                        "task has errored out after {}!".format(task_time))
        elif self.on_exit:
            # Tick notification icon?
            ntfy.notify("Task: {}".format(self.task_name),
                        "task has finished in {}!".format(task_time))
        # if self.suppress_error: return true ?
```

It would also simplify the whole run-a-process command already within the library (not that it isn't already)

``` python
def for_a_process_thing(commandline):
    with TaskNotifier(commandline):
        subprocess.check_call(commandline)
```

However this is just a silly idea, a tad bit complicated for what it does, and might not be worth the maintenance cost! but if you like the idea, I'd be happy to implement it for you

issue_title: Converting concentrations from mass/L to mol/L
issue_body:
Greetings.

I have a question that might help me start researching a solution to a thorny problem in the conversion of lab test results between the two sets of units most commonly used.

In the medical field it's fairly common to test the concentration of salts such as Calcium or Sodium in fluids like blood or spinal fluid. Two common units used to express these measurements are something like mg/dL, and mmol/dL, for example. The problem, of course, is that for a given concentration of a salt in mg/dL, the conversion to mmol/dL is dependent on the molecular weight of the salt involved. So the coefficient will be different for Calcium than for Sodium, and so on.

How would we best achieve this? I could imagine introducing constants to express the molecular weights for each of the elements we're expecting to encounter. We could brute-force the conversion by specifying units for each element: mgCa, mmolCa, mgNa, mmolNa, and so on, I guess. Any ideas for addressing this more cleanly?

Thanks,
Jon Blackburn

References/examples:
http://www-users.med.cornell.edu/~spon/picu/calc/cacalc.htm

issue_title: SAM Header interspersed through file
issue_body:
Hi Heng, 

Just came across a bizarre issue which I have been debugging all day to try and get at the root cause. 

To cut a long story short, it looks like in some circumstances the SAM header gets spread out in chunks throughout the SAM file - almost like there are multiple SAM files concatenated together.

I only see this when aligning to a reference that is effectively a database of fasta files concatenated together. 

For example: 

```
minimap2 -a DB.fasta my.fastq > my.sam
``` 

I have a file `DB.fasta` which has 16498 entries in it. When I align my fastq file to this reference the resulting SAM file has 4 separate header locations spread throughout the file. Each section is unique. 

To check my sanity I ran the exact same thing with `bwa mem` and there are no issues.   
Also, if I just align the sample to a single reference (with `minimap2`) such as GRCh38, the resulting SAM is fine.

When trying to view the resulting SAM file in `samtools view` I get an error saying the file is truncated. Therefore I was only able to see this in `vim`.

My current install of `minimap2` is `2.0-r191-dirty`

Let me know if you want some more information.

issue_title: Problem with RDY locking to zero
issue_body:
Hi Trevor! First of all -- thank you so much for all your hard work on gnsq. It's a lovely little library and for the most part has been absolutely flawless for us.

Unfortunately we're having an issue at the moment with gnsq v0.2.3 (and nsqd v0.3.0) in which it seems like a network blip causes a `Reader` to lock itself in a ready state of zero and never recover.

We're getting the following error message in the logs:

```
[172.17.11.122:4150] error requeueing message (NSQSocketError(32, 'Broken pipe'))
```

This appears to come from [line 699 of `reader.py`](https://github.com/wtolson/gnsq/blob/3a6016/gnsq/reader.py#L699), and seems to me to imply that we're having a network blip while attempting a requeue. Thereafter, it seems like `RDY` never increments again. You can see the issue where we're dealing with this internally at hypothesis/h#2304.

I've tried to recreate the conditions under which this might occur using [blockade](https://github.com/dcm-oss/blockade) (see [this repo](https://github.com/nickstenning/gnsq-blockade)), but as yet haven't succeeded.

I've perused the source code, and I'm guessing this might be the issue you fixed in d193160e4f0b37bf4214d1128b476a3f366454c4 (and 6905a1449fd84afd56b5417681e372bfb6fb6081). Does that sound right?

If not, is there any way I can help track this issue down?

I've just given it a try by installing the GitHub master, and it installed just fine via `python3 setup install`, and the 11 unit tests also ran fine using `nosetests3`.

Personally, I have only done a very brief test of the module itself, but it did import in the interactive Python3 shell, and getting random numbers has worked as well.

According to the revision history, a commit `5cd2f4a20d72fb31655908d0e6a05c6204abe4ab` was supposed to provide some Py3 support.

It would be nice if this package could also be made available to PyPI, which currently only hosts version 0.6.5.1 for Python 2.x.

issue_title: conn.modify documentation issue
issue_body:
It looks like there is a documentation error on this page:
http://ldap3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/connections.html

Specifically, the changes structure for the modify operation is listed as:

    {‘attribute1’: [(operation, [val1, val2, ...])], ‘attribute2’: [(operation, [val1, val2, ...])]}

This does not work, giving `ldap3.core.exceptions.LDAPChangesError: malformed change`. Looking at the test code, it should be:

    {‘attribute1’: (operation, [val1, val2, ...]), ‘attribute2’: (operation, [val1, val2, ...])}

Thanks!
(There operation Tuple should not be enclosed in a list.)

issue_title: Kodi now working
issue_body:
Notifications are not working on Kodi 17 (Krypton).  Here is the command and error message:

```
apprise -t 'my title' -b 'my notification body' 'kodi://user:pass@kodi:8080'
```

```
17:15:14.490 T:2424492960   ERROR: JSONRPC: Failed to parse '{"id":1,"image":"http://nuxref.com/apprise/themes/default/apprise-info-128x128.png","jsonrpc":"6.0","method":"GUI.ShowNotification","params":{"displaytime":12000,"message":"my notification body","title":"my title"},"type":"info"}'
```

Changing jsonrpc from 6.0 to 2.0 seems to do the trick.  I am not sure if this would break older and EOL versions of Kodi.  Is backward compatibility important?    The kodi team does not plan another 17 release and 18 (Leia) alpha 1 was released in March. 

Also FYI, the apprise image is not being displayed in the notification, but the theme info icon is.  I am not sure if this is due to Kodi's themes I tested, including the default.  Personally, I prefer the icon over the image.

issue_title: dnn_face_recognition_ex face descriptor normalization
issue_body:
Hi,

I have a question about the dnn architecture employed in the [dnn_face_recognition_ex.cpp](https://github.com/davisking/dlib/blob/master/examples/dnn_face_recognition_ex.cpp) example. It looks like the output descriptors are normalized, as the they contain values between -1 and 1. 

However, I cannot see any kind of normalization applied, as the last layer is a fully connected layer. Is softmax or something applied under the hood? Because when I export the net to a caffe model, with the tool you provided, the last layer is a simple InnerProduct layer, which doesn't seem to do any kind of normalization on the final vector:
`n.fc_no_bias1 = L.InnerProduct(n.avg_pool2, num_output=128, bias_term=False);`

Sorry if this is kind of a noob question, but I am new to all the deep learning, caffe and tensorflow mess and I am trying to understand the architecture of your net.

The reason I am asking is, I am trying to port the architecture to tensorflow.js, in order to run face-recognition in the browser via WebGL, but I am trying to figure out how to build the output layer.

Thanks in advance for your help, I would highly appreciate your advice.

issue_title: Bug in merge_points
issue_body:
Hi Michael,

yesterday I came across your code because I have to merge a lot of points of a surface triangulation. After some testing I came across a bug in the "merge_vertices()" function of your code.
Here is a minimal example:

import numpy as np
import trimesh
nds2 = np.array([[ 1.        ,  1.60994057,  2.07798185],
                 [ 1.        ,  0.60994057,  2.07798184],
                 [ 1.        ,  0.60994057,  2.07798185]])
ele2 = np.array([[0,1,2]])
mesh = trimesh.Trimesh(vertices=nds2, faces=ele2,process=True)
mesh.merge_vertices()
print mesh.vertices
print np.linalg.norm(mesh.vertices[1]-mesh.vertices[2])

Best,
Konrad

issue_title: [deviantart] Add support for Groups galleries
issue_body:
`gallery-dl --version`
> 0.9.1-dev

OS: Windows 10 CU x64
Python 3.6.1. x64

Found something on DeviantArt (again) 😄 
Groups..

1: Home URL of a group
`http://cgpinups.deviantart.com/`
```
PS D:\Stuff> gallery-dl -v http://cgpinups.deviantart.com/
[gallery-dl][debug] Starting DownloadJob for 'http://cgpinups.deviantart.com/'
[deviantart][debug] Using DeviantartGalleryExtractor for http://cgpinups.deviantart.com/
[urllib3.connectionpool][debug] Starting new HTTPS connection (1): www.deviantart.com
[urllib3.connectionpool][debug] https://www.deviantart.com:443 "GET /api/v1/oauth2/gallery/all?username=cgpinups&offset=0&limit=10&mature_content=true HTTP/1.1" 200 70
PS D:\Stuff>
```

2: Gallery URL of group
`http://cgpinups.deviantart.com/gallery/`

Same result as above.

3: Gallery Folders in the Group (The actual galleries, so to speak) (One example of them)
`http://cgpinups.deviantart.com/gallery/25871850/Fantasy-and-Sci-Fi`
```
[gallery-dl][error] No suitable extractor found for 'http://cgpinups.deviantart.com/gallery/25871850/Fantasy-and-Sci-Fi'
```

I think the question is how the API handles this stuff.
It doesn't make sense to me right now, but I guess this is related to the difference between _Gallery_ and _Favourites_. If this is the same distinction by the API...

For example, a user's gallery has _Gallery Folders_ (just as a group), while the user's favourites has _collections_.

Gallery folders also don't work.
Example:
```
PS D:\Stuff> gallery-dl "http://arsenixc.deviantart.com/gallery/11314091/Backgrounds"
[gallery-dl][error] No suitable extractor found for 'http://arsenixc.deviantart.com/gallery/11314091/Backgrounds'
PS D:\Stuff>
```

No point in having two separate issues here, I think. Depends on the API results, I guess.

issue_title: Unicode: encoding detection doesn't work
issue_body:
The solution proposed for issue #25 does not, in fact, accurately detect file format when BOM is absent.

If the 32 first characters are pure ASCII, then 'ascii' encoding is "detected". When other characters are present, *an* encoding compatible with that character is chosen, but it needs not be the correct one. 

For instance, with character `é` present in the first line, I get encoding 'cp-1252' on Windows, while other characters are present later in the file that can't be represented in that encoding (box drawing characters).

The classical coding specification `-*- coding: utf-8 -*-` is not considered at all.

issue_title: improve unexpected error handling
Restructure evaluators and io requirements
issue_body:
Historically, `mir_eval` has required that for each task there exists a data loader which loads in data from some filetype to be used in the task.  These data loaders exist in the `io` submodule.  For example, for beat detection, [data has historically been formatted as an ASCII file where each beat location (in seconds) appears on each line](http://www.music-ir.org/mirex/wiki/2017:Audio_Beat_Tracking#Output_Data), and the function [`mir_eval.io.load_events`](https://craffel.github.io/mir_eval/#mir_eval.io.load_events) implements this.  There are a few things which prompted this requirement:
1. Each task should have an "evaluator" which is a Python script, useable as a binary, which takes in paths to reference and estimated annotation files and outputs scores for that task.
1. The [`mir_eval` web service](https://labrosa.ee.columbia.edu/mir_eval/) requires that people can upload annotation files for their task in order to receive scores.
1. The docstring of each evaluation function in each task should have an example for calling the function, including loading in data.
1. The regression tests require parsing existing data files, and so including input/output functionality has allowed us to keep external test-only dependencies down.

In a [recent survey of `mir_eval` users](https://goo.gl/forms/XjPnGJxr8f2oQtsR2), the vast majority (97.4%) said they had used `mir_eval` by calling it directly from Python code.  About 12.8% said they used the evaluators, and no respondent had used the web service.  Similarly, 81.6% said they had used a separate data loading procedure; 26.3% said they had used functions in the `io` submodule, and 7.9% said data was loaded because they were using the evaluator.

Requiring that every task has a data loading function has some drawbacks.
1. Some tasks don't have a pre-existing data loading format, so we've had to make one up.  It seems silly to make up a format just to satisfy the requirements of `mir_eval`'s conventions.
1. In some cases, the format supported by `mir_eval` is not always obviously the best format for the task.  For example, for transcription, MIDI is a pretty nice format.  One could argue that [JAMS](https://github.com/marl/jams) is better than all of the annotation formats we support.
1. There already exist good (better?) Python data loaders than the ones we implemented.  For example, `librosa` would work well for loading in wav files for source separation evaluation.
1. Following the principle of "do one thing and do it well", `mir_eval` should implement evaluation functions, not implement data loading/parsing and a set of scripts for using `mir_eval`.

Given that the `mir_eval` web service is not used, the evaluators are sparsely used, and using the `io` submodule is much less common than using another input pipeline, I propose the following changes:
1. Deprecate support for the `mir_eval` web service and move it to a separate repository.  Treat it as a "proof of concept" instead of a supported project.
1. Move the evaluators to a separate repository. Do not require that every task has an evaluator, but support the existing evaluators which have tasks.
1. Do not require that each task have a data loading function in `io`.  In the future, only implement data loading functions which are genuinely useful because they don't already exist in other software and which are for widely used annotation formats (e.g. those used in MIREX).
1. Because of the above point, do not require that each example in each docstring includes data loading.  Only include an example when it is useful for seeing how data should be loaded or preprocessed (for example, in the `beat` submodule where beats should be trimmed before being passed to evaluation functions).

In order to accommodate the new repositories, I propose that we move http://github.com/craffel/mir_eval to http://github.com/mir_eval/mir_eval and create repositories http://github.com/mir_eval/web_service and http://github.com/mir_eval/evaluators for the web service and evaluators respectively.

If there are no major objections, I will start implementing these moves and changes next week.

issue_title: Issue with parser cache and (nested?) namespace packages
issue_body:
I've noticed that Jedi fails to find definitions in a particular setup, which is related to cached parsers.
It looks like something gets cached in a wrong way (and `jedi.cache.clear_time_caches(True)` fixes it).

```
% virtualenv venv-django_filter
% source venv-django_filter/bin/activate
% pip install django_filter==0.15
% python django_filter.py
```

``` python
import jedi
# import inspect
# print(inspect.getfile(jedi))

jedi.settings.use_filesystem_cache = False

source = """import django_filters
django_filters.rest_framework.filters.OrderingFilter()
"""

print("=== without path ===")
script = jedi.Script(source, column=42, line=2)
d1 = script.goto_definitions()
assert len(d1)
print("=== without path again ===")
script = jedi.Script(source, column=42, line=2)
d2 = script.goto_definitions()
assert len(d2)
```

I've bisected this and found that with the following commit the following assertion fails already (also in v0.9.0):

commit 8fca3f7 (HEAD, refs/bisect/bad)
Date:   Tue Apr 14 17:36:20 2015 +0200

```
Add a py__name__ call to modules. This makes listing the qualified names of modules possible (in combination with the module_name_cache). Fixes #519.
```

`django_filters/rest_framework/filters.py` ([source](https://github.com/carltongibson/django-filter/blob/73b2228d777d9d97ed622b9b14a6110bf5ba4c7f/django_filters/rest_framework/filters.py)) uses:

``` python
from ..filters import *
```

This was changed in the following commit.  Since then the 2nd assertion fails:

commit ec51891 (refs/bisect/bad)
Date:   Sat Jul 30 02:44:09 2016 +0200

```
Fix nested namespace packages. At least now there's no error anymore. Fixes #743.
```

issue_title: Wrong and missing match equivalents
issue_body:
Looking at the matching equivalencies, I found [this](https://github.com/Diaoul/subliminal/blob/master/subliminal/score.py#L107):
```
        if 'tvdb_id' in matches:
            logger.debug('Adding tvdb_id match equivalents')
            matches |= {'series', 'year'}
```

Since `tvdb_id` is the episode tvdb_id and `series_tvdb_id` is the series tvdb_id, I believe that the correct would be:
```
        if 'tvdb_id' in matches:
            logger.debug('Adding tvdb_id match equivalents')
            matches |= {'series', 'year', 'season', 'episode'}
        if 'series_tvdb_id' in matches:
            logger.debug('Adding series_tvdb_id match equivalents')
            matches |= {'series', 'year'}
```

And just as an additional note, after analyzing thousands of release names from episodes, I noticed these standards:
 - The vast majority of the release names contains season and episode
 - A minor part of them contains an `absolute episode number` or `parts` (sometimes with season, sometimes with an year) - Probably that should be also equivalent to season + episode match
 - Some few cases you have only air date (probably air date match should be equivalent to season + episode match)

```py

issue_title: Handle images more like the LaTeX Sphinx builder?
issue_body:
Some of the images in the [Lizmap documentation](/3liz/lizmap-documentation) are displayed much larger than in the LaTeX-generated PDF.

Notes:
- this is perhaps only the case for images that do not have a DPI set
- LaTeX's `\includegraphics` or Sphinx's `\sphinxincludegraphics` may always scale down the image if it doesn't fit the available width
- setting an `.. image:`'s `:scale: 80%` seems to scale the image to 80% of the text width (**not** 80% of the image's original size)

While it is convenient to have the images fit the available width if their are too wide, it is probably not a good idea to scale relative to the text width. If supported by Sphinx/docutils, the user should set `:width: 80%`.

UnboundLocalError thrown for certain timeout values
issue_body:
Guys - quick question about some strange behavior we are seeing on the consumer side.

We made the following change, in particular dropping out `session_time_ms`:
`@@ -44,7 +44,7 @@ def get_kafka_consumer(topic,group_id):
                              api_version=KAFKA_API_VERSION,
                              auto_offset_reset='latest',
                              enable_auto_commit=False,
-                             session_timeout_ms=300000)
+                             session_timeout_ms=5000)

Which resulted in our code throwing the following error:
`Exception in thread Thread-3:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib64/python2.7/threading.py", line 810, in __bootstrap_inner
    self.run()
  File "/usr/lib64/python2.7/threading.py", line 763, in run

    return func.__get__(obj, cls)
UnboundLocalError: <unprintable UnboundLocalError object>`

Looking at the code that throws the error:
`def _unpickle_method(func_name, obj, cls):
    for cls in cls.mro():
        try:
            func = cls.__dict__[func_name]
        except KeyError:
            pass
        else:

        return func.__get__(obj, cls)`

I assume that a `KeyError` causes execution to fall through without binding a value for `func`. Why does a timeout, or setting a time value too low, cause an `UnboundLocalError`?

I would like to help fix, but not sure what the cause is.

-Oscar

issue_title: Recommend Brotli instead of brotlipy?
issue_body:
When Brotli support was first added to WhiteNoise, if I remember correctly, the official brotli repo did not yet have its own Python package/bindings. As such, the WhiteNoise docs recommend using [brotlipy](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/brotlipy).

However there's now the official Python package, [Brotli](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/Brotli), which I've been using with WhiteNoise for a while.

Unless there are good reasons to stick with the unofficial bindings, I think we should update the WhiteNoise docs to point people at the `Brotli` package instead.

issue_title: Remove `Pipfile.lock` from repository
issue_body:
`pipenv` docs states in [General Recommendations & Version Control](https://docs.pipenv.org/basics/#general-recommendations-version-control):

> Do not keep Pipfile.lock in version control if multiple versions of Python are being targeted.

As `maya` is python library targeting multiple python versions, we should follow this advice.

# Reasoning

Researching this topic, I would summarize the reasoning as follows:
- `maya` is python package targeting multiple OSes and different python interpreter versions.
- `Pipfile.lock` reflects frozen set of dependent package versions created for given creation context (OS, python version...)
- Such `Pipfile.lock` may be simply broken for different context. As it does not contain markers, it is not usable for alternative contexts.
- The only source of truth for dependent packages (in case of a library) can be just `Pipfile`, which is likely to be projected into requirements within `setup.py` (or within `requirements.txt` if one is using a solution such as `pbr`).
- The `Pipfile` shall provide be strict enough in all cases where we know, things must be that way, but in all other cases it shall allow some degree of freedom. This allows to make the package well behaving in context of other packages (imagine a project, where two different python packages would prescribe exact package version for all dependency and some would be conflicting).

# Things to do
If removal of `Pipfile.lock` is accepted, we should:

- remove the `Pipfile.lock` from repository
- add `Pipfile.lock` into `.gitignore`
- modify `tox.ini` not to rely on `Pipfile.lock`, so the [line 7 in `tox.ini`](https://github.com/kennethreitz/maya/blob/master/tox.ini#L7) shall read `pipenv install --dev --skip-lock`

issue_title: Problems loading nested dicts with Pyrebase?
issue_body:
Hi!

Trying to push nested dicts using Pyrebase unsuccessfully. My data structure should be like the following:
```
ids
  |
  --- record1
                 |
                 --- attribute1
                 |
                 --- attribute2
                 |
                 --- dictofattributes
                                            |
                                            --- nestedattribute1
                                            |
                                            --- nestedattribute2
  
```
That is what I present to pyrebase as data format, called `dbdata`. Be aware that the whole data structure is presented to the Pyrebase wrapper as a Python dict, were `dictofattributes` is a nested dict.

What I do to push the data into Pyrebase is:
```
db.child("id").push(dbdata, user['idToken'])
```
If I try the nested dict, I get the following error:
```
Invalid data; couldn't parse JSON object, array, or value.
```

I have tried several things including converting the nested dict into a json file and then reconverting it into a dict before pushing, assuming it is a format problem. Those solutions haven't worked. However if I pass the nested dict as a list of nested lists instead, it passes without conflict.

+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
Make sure these boxes are checked before submitting your issue:

[x] Check that your version of Python is 3.4+
[x] Check that you are on the newest version of Pyrebase
[x] Check that Email/password provider is enabled in your Firebase dashboard under Auth -> Sign In Method.

Please don't be discouraged if you do not get a response to your issue quickly,
I maintain Pyrebase for fun and don't always have as much free time as I'd like.

Thank you for helping make Pyrebase better!

issue_body:
I'm trying to integrate word_cloud into my app, but the resulting images seem to be consistently misleading.

I've attached a file with simple python code and the resulting image.  Looking at the code, you'll see that the two most frequent words are "this" and "romney", both with a frequency of 5 occurrences.  So I would expect them to be represented as words in the same font size, or perhaps with the same overall area in the display, or at least some way that would visually indicate the "sameness" of their frequency.  Instead, the word "this" is presented with font_size of 470 and "romney" with font_size of 220, so that "this" looks MUCH more important than "romney."

"what", "here", "question", "we're", "crowley", and "have" all have a frequency of 4, but font sizes varying from 152 to 58 (and not correlated with word length).

David

issue_title: Downloaded files will not play on Win32 - Workaround for Win32
issue_body:
### What version of Python are you using (`python -V`)?
Python 2.7.12

### What operating system and processor architecture are you using (`python -c 'import platform; print(platform.uname());'`)?
python -c "import platform; print(platform.uname());"
('Windows', 'AK_i7', '7', '6.1.7601', 'AMD64', 'Intel64 Family 6 Model 58 Stepping 9, GenuineIntel')

### Which Python packages do you have installed (run the `pip freeze` or `pip3 freeze` command and paste output)?
E:\arlo\examples>pip freeze
arlo==1.0.0
bottle==0.12.9
build==1.0.2
click==5.1
colorama==0.3.7
docutils==0.12
Kivy==1.9.0
Kivy-Garden==0.1.4
lockfile==0.12.2
monotonic==1.4
mysql-connector-python==2.1.2
platformio==2.11.2
pycairo==1.8.10
pyglet==1.2.4
Pygments==2.0.2
pygobject==2.28.3
pygtk==2.24.0
pyserial==3.1.1
requests==2.11.0
six==1.11.0
sseclient==0.0.18
UNKNOWN==0.0.0
You are using pip version 8.1.2, however version 9.0.1 is available.
You should consider upgrading via the 'python -m pip install --upgrade pip' command.

### Which Arlo hardware do you have (camera types - [Arlo, Pro, Q, etc.], basestation model, etc.)?
Basic Arlo Camera

### What did you do?
Downloaded files would not play.

For those that may have a similar issue using win32 (specifically Windows 7 Pro, but probably all windows OS version Python 2.7 will run on), here is a simple workaround.  Credits to: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/38404827/avoid-writing-carriage-return-when-writing-line-feed-with-python [UBICA]

Sorry, I'm not familiar with Python -- I usually use perl for scripting, so this is probably not an optimal solution.

Changed line 36 "with open(videopath + '/' + videofilename, 'w') as f:"
to:  "with open(videopath + '/' + videofilename, 'wb') as f:"

Apparently opening in the binary mode forces windows to interpret data literally -- does not replace LF wioth CRLF.

### What did you expect to see?
LF only was expected in downloaded file

### What did you see instead?
Found CRLF (classic windoze vs xNIX issue)

### Does this issue reproduce with the latest release?
Assuming this is generic to any win32 OS and current example scripts.

Downloaded files play as well as files saved from the ARLO App.  Thanks so much for making ARLO useful for me!  Not having an archive > 7 days caused loss of useful data (people stealing hose in front yard - then oddly, replacing it!).  Now I can archive videos!

issue_title: Type hinting
issue_body:
I have started to use `mypy`. Aaaand it is not so good right now.

Libraries lack typing support. And I know that `mimesis` support only `python3`.
Do you consider adding type hints?

There are some advantages:
- stricter API
- static analysis support to improve quality

There are some disadvantages:
- type hinting was introduced in `python3.5`, so any version prior to that will have to use special comments or other hacks
- a lot of manual work
- possible API changes

issue_title: Overwriting the last working dir
issue_body:
I have a trouble with [last-working-dir](https://github.com/robbyrussell/oh-my-zsh/blob/master/plugins/last-working-dir/last-working-dir.plugin.zsh) zsh plugin. This plugin absolutely doesn't work with `autoenv` because it overwrites the last working directory here: [activate.sh#L29](https://github.com/kennethreitz/autoenv/blob/master/activate.sh#L29).

The `autoenv` overwrites last working directory to `/home` after each `cd` call, so all new terminal instances will always open with `/home` working directory.

I have a temporary fix:
```sh
autoenv_cd() {
  if builtin cd "$@"
  then
    cache_file="$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/cache/last-working-dir"
    last_working_dir="`cat "$cache_file"`"

    autoenv_init

    echo $last_working_dir >| "$cache_file"

    return 0
  else
    return $?
  fi
}
``` 

But I think we should completely rewrite this place: [activate.sh#L22](https://github.com/kennethreitz/autoenv/blob/master/activate.sh#L22) in such way:
```ruby
require 'pathname'

def config_dir(dir = Pathname.new("."))
  app_config_dir = dir + ".myappconfigfolder"
  if dir.children.include?(app_config_dir)
    app_config_dir.expand_path
  else
    return nil if dir.expand_path.root?
    config_dir(dir.parent)
  end
end
```
[Source](https://www.practicingruby.com/articles/configurable-applications-2)

This codein Ruby  doing a reverse search from the current working directory back to the filesystem’s root directory. As for me, it is better way to solve this issue, but I haven't enough experience to implement this in the shell script.

issue_title: Document save func says to use .path() instead of .patch()
issue_body:
save(waitForSync=False, **docArgs)[source]
Saves the document to the database by either performing a POST (for a new document) or a PUT (complete document overwrite). If you want to only update the modified fields use the **.path()** function. Use docArgs to put things such as ‘waitForSync = True’ (for a full list cf ArangoDB’s doc). It will only trigger a saving of the document if it has been modified since the last save. If you want to force the saving you can use forceSave()

issue_title: Validation errors nested in lists are broken and raise AttributeError
issue_body:
Between 0.8.9 and 0.8.10, it is no longer possible to correctly validate the content of lists. It seems that before the latest commits the inner exceptions were wrapped into a specific exception type, but now they may be propagated as MultipleInvalid exceptions. This exception type will not allow setting its `path` property, so it may cause the following error:

```python
>>> from voluptuous import *
>>> s = Schema(All([Any('one', 'two', 'three')]), Length(min=1))
>>> s(['one'])
['one']
>>> s(['four'])
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/voluptuous.py", line 351, in __call__
    return self._compiled([], data)
  File "venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/voluptuous.py", line 793, in validate_callable
    error.path = path + error.path
AttributeError: can't set attribute
```

If I run from 32aeeec65d77940655a2905c9f15114586eb785e I get the correct:

```python
>>> s(['four'])
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/voluptuous.py", line 355, in __call__
    return self._compiled([], data)
  File "venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/voluptuous.py", line 794, in validate_callable
    return schema(data)
  File "venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/voluptuous.py", line 1394, in __call__
    raise e if self.msg is None else AllInvalid(self.msg)
voluptuous.MultipleInvalid: invalid list value @ data[0]
```

issue_title: Don't use mutables as argument defaults in functions
issue_body:
https://github.com/veox/python3-krakenex/blob/master/krakenex/api.py#L151
When defining functions like this `def query_private(self, method, req={}, conn = None):` you share a single instance of the `req` dictionary for every call that uses the default of `req`.

```python
def fn(x={}):
    return x

call1 = fn()
print(call1)
call1["a"] = "A"
call2 = fn()
print(call2)
print (call1 == call2)
```
If you want a fresh instance of an dictionary on every call of the function I suggest something like this.

```python
def fn(x=None):
    if x is None:
        x = {}
```

Nevertheless I don't see a concrete problem raised by this in your code.

issue_title: Remove `*.dist-info` to get stable CodeSha256
issue_body:
I would very much like to use the CodeSha256 attribute to check whether multiple stages are running identical code.

Unfortunately, every time I do `sls deploy`, even to the same stage, the generated zip file has a slightly different content and therefore different hash.

This turns out to be due to changes in the `*.dist-info` from pip packages added by `serverless-wsgi` - details below.  Stripping this out makes the sha256 stable.

Since the dist-info data is not required for successful running of the library, please could `serverless-wsgi` filter it out by default?

issue_title: TypeError on admin changelist
issue_body:
Hello,

When visiting the changelist page of redisboard in admin I get

```TypeError: a float is required```

the error happens exactly on line 165 in [here](https://github.com/ionelmc/django-redisboard/blob/master/src/redisboard/models.py#L165).

The reason for the error is because at this point the values of ```ts```, ```id```, ```command```, etc.. are the keys of the dictionary in https://github.com/andymccurdy/redis-py/blob/master/redis/client.py#L272 instead of the actual values because of the ```enumerate``` call.

issue_title: Adding env REF to specify the source
issue_body:
Current `rpm` master branch show version like this.

```
$ dest/bin/rpm --version
RPM version 4.14.90
```

But there is no archive here for that.
https://github.com/rpm-software-management/rpm/releases

The solution is to provide the way for users to specify commit reference (commit hash) as argument option of environment variable.

```
REF=4adc4f198cbc59b493a5f8a92e6e85b95c29719f or 4adc4f1 or master pip install rpm-py-installer
```

The test failed for current rawhide by this reason.
https://travis-ci.org/junaruga/rpm-py-installer/jobs/274206985

```
$ git clone REPO_URL
$ git checkout REF
```

issue_title: Aggregating transactional data neglects first-day purchases
issue_body:
Hello,
I want to point out an issue regarding the data aggregator:

```from lifetimes.utils import summary_data_from_transaction_data```

If you aggregate data with the Days (D) frequency (which is the default) the users that do purchase the first day, just after the install, result in zero frequency in the aggregated dataset.
So we are neglecting a possibly large fraction of purchasing users, considering them as non-purchasing.

If you want an example, consider this transactional data:
```id,date,monetary_value
1,2016-01-01 00:01:44,0.0
2,2016-01-01 00:01:50,0.0
1,2016-01-01 00:01:55,5.0
1,2016-01-01 00:01:58,5.0
2,2016-01-05 00:01:50,1.0
```
Run the aggregator on them:
``` from lifetimes.utils import summary_data_from_transaction_data
data = summary_data_from_transaction_data(transaction_data, 'id', 'date',monetary_value_col='monetary_value', observation_period_end='2016-04-08', freq='D')
print data.head()```
You'll get:
```
  frequency  recency     T  monetary_value
id                                          
1         0.0      0.0  98.0             0.0
2         1.0      4.0  98.0             1.0
```
Which is plain wrong in my opinion.
The purchases that user 1 does the first day are completely neglected.
How to fix this?

Cheers
Marco

issue_title: use_virtual not obeyed in midiutil.open_midiinput if interactive=true
issue_body:
Hello,
thanks for this nice library!
The documentation for rtmidi.midiutil.open_midiport says:

> If port is None or no MIDI port matching the port number or name is available, should the user be prompted on the console whether to open a virtual MIDI port (if use_virtual is True) and/or with a list of available MIDI ports and the option to choose one? Defaults to True.

So I expected midiutil.open_midiinput(use_virtual=False, interactive=True) NOT to ask if I'd like to use a virtual port, but it does anyway.

In my opinion it but be great if use_virtual could be something like None or "prompt" (then ask), otherwise it should obey use_virtual=True or use_virtual=False.

What do you think?
Best, Boris

issue_title: Failed initialisation if other app provides jquery after redactor.min.js is included
issue_body:
In some cases, when you use an app thet provides an own version of jquery, the redactor widget fails to initialize.
The line 
```javascript
$('#textarea').redactor(...)
```
Throws an exception: ```Uncaught TypeError: undefined is not a function ```

You could solve this by ovveriding the ```change_form.html``` admin template:

```html
{% extends "admin/change_form.html" %}

{% block extrahead %}{{ block.super }}
<!-- append redactor js to the end of the head segment -->
<script type="text/javascript" src="/static/suit-redactor/redactor/redactor.min.js"></script>
{% endblock %}
```

The caveat of that is that the js is included in the template twice (and possibly interpreted twice), but it makes things work again.

Or if there is the possibility to tell other apps to abstain from adding its own jquery, you could do that. In my specific example, this is the case for https://github.com/asyncee/django-easy-select2. There you can set the option
```python
SELECT2_USE_BUNDLED_JQUERY = False
```

The issue is caused because django-admins jquery is loaded before redactor.min.js and the other apps jquery after redactor.min.js, wich overrides that binding.

This is not really a bug, but worth a mention in the docs.

issue_title: subscribing to an empty set kills pubsub
issue_body:
This appears to be a bug, but I'll leave that to the public to decide.

When subscribing to nothing, the pubsub stops listening completely.

```
subscriber.subscribe([])
subscriber.subscribe(['my_channel'])
```

After the first statement, `subscriber.next_published()` will never hear another reply.

This doesn't appear to be documented on [Redis Subscribe](http://redis.io/commands/subscribe) so I'm assuming this is a bug?

issue_title: Initialize asyncio reactor with preconfigured ThreadPoolExecutor to control thread counts
issue_body:
Daphne currently initializes an asyncio reactor with no default executor, so when something like aioredis is used (as it is in redis_channels), a default ThreadPoolExecutor is created on first execute call which has cpu_count * 5 threads as default (which is suboptimal for many deployments).

Consider adding something like the following to daphne's server.py where plain asyncioreactor.install() is being used now:

from asyncio.events import new_event_loop
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor

    event_loop = new_event_loop()
    threadpool = ThreadPoolExecutor(
        max_workers=(
            int(os.environ["ASIO_THREADS"])
            if "ASIO_THREADS" in os.environ
            else None
        )
    )
    event_loop.set_default_executor(threadpool)
    asyncioreactor.install(event_loop)

When testing on OSX, I was able to control the asyncioreactor thread pool size using the ASIO_THREADS env variable with this code.

issue_title: `directory_regex` support (or something similar)?
issue_body:
I have users of our SOPS implementation that would prefer to use subdirectories to determine what encryption keys to use.  Our project structure is as follows:

```
├── engineering
├── ops
├── resources
└── ssl
```

Right now, we have a .sops.yaml file in each directory and require users to cd to the directory before creating a new file.  I get a lot of forgetful users who will create secrets from the top level.

So instead of

we get

If I could extend the configuration featureset to include a `directory_regex` I can run a regex on the first filename that is checked and see if it matches.  If no match, pass through to the `filename_regex` as it works today.

```
creation_rules:
  - directory_regex: ^engineering/
    kms: '...'
    pgp: '...'
  - directory_regex: ^ops/
    kms: '...'

happy to submit a patch if you are interested in accepting

issue_title: Add documentation on GPU vs CPU speed
Serialize tuples (wavID, resultText, logProbability) for test, dev, and train sets from trained model
issue_body:
The DeepSpeech paper[[1](https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.5567)] indicates that they maximized
```
Q(c) = log(P_n(c|x)) + a·log(P_l(c)) +   b·word_count(c)
```
where `a` and `b` are hyperparameters, `P_n(c|x)` is the probability of the string `c` given the feature vector `x`, while `P_l(c)` is the probability of the string `c` according to the language model, and `word_count(c)` is the number of words in `c`.

To adjust the hyperparameters `a` and `b` we can use the serialized tuples `(wavID, resultText, logProbability)` the adjust `a` and `b` offline to minimize `Q(c)`. This should be a simple machine learning model in which the parameters `a` and `b` are the only things that need to be learned and the data set if the serialized tuples.

Note: In the tuple it's obvious what `resultText` and `logProbability` are; `wavID` is less obvious. However, it's fine to have `wavID` be the index of the wav file in the epoch.

    name='Extra IP Addresses',
    unit_amount_in_cents=200
)
print addon1
<recurly.AddOn object at 0x7f05b52da510>
```

However, when you call `plan.create_add_on(addon1)`, it errors due to `unit_amount_in_cents`:
```python

In attempt to fix this issue, use an integer when creating the `SubscriptionAddOn` as shown in docs:

my_sub.subscription_add_ons = []

issue_title: framing with "@embed": "@link" cannot handle circular references
issue_body:
Framing with "@embed": "@link" has been proposed as one way to create an in-memory representation of a graph (as discussed in json-ld/json-ld.org#140 ).

jsonld.js does this in a way that handles circular references, but when I try to use pyld to frame:

```json
{
  "@context": "http://schema.org/",
  "@type": "Person",
  "name": "Jane Doe",
  "jobTitle": "Professor",
  "telephone": "(425) 123-4567",
  "@id": "http://www.janedoe.com",
  "knows": {
    "name": "John Smith",
    "@type": "Person",
    "@id": "http://www.johnsmith.me",
    "knows": {
      "@id": "http://www.janedoe.com"
    }
  }
}
```

with a frame like:
```json
{
  "@context": "http://schema.org",
  "@embed": "@link"
}
```

... I get a RuntimeError from exceeding the recursion limit.

I also demonstrate this in [a gist](https://gist.github.com/bpow/b3af1e454c51fae7b7c2d1e4980da408)

issue_title: Union trait does not handle trait metadata correctly
issue_body:
If I have a `HasTraits` class, called `Mix`, that has a `Union` trait, called `union`, then since the trait metadata of `union` is a class attribute, all `Mix` instances will have the same metadata for `union`, regardless of the actual trait type of `union`.

This can be harmful if you have a `Union` of traits with different `to_json` for example.

I hope this will be clearer with an example:
```python
from traitlets import Float, Unicode, HasTraits

class Mix(HasTraits):
    union = Float().tag(metadata="I'm a float") | Unicode().tag(metadata="I'm still a float, but with unicode metadata")
    
mix1 = Mix(union=0)
print mix1.union, mix1.trait_metadata('union', 'metadata')
mix2 = Mix(union='')
print mix1.union, mix1.trait_metadata('union', 'metadata')
```
outputs
```python
0.0 I'm a float
0.0 I'm still a float, but with unicode metadata
```

issue_title: Crash VCSA (vxpd?) when using AddStandaloneHost_Task() on rootFolder
issue_body:
By accident I discovered that one can crash the vxpd service by doing this:

    task = self.content.rootFolder.AddStandaloneHost_Task(host_connect_spec, compute_resource_spec, as_connected, license)

Where:

    compute_resource_spec = None
    license = None

If you do this (correctly) on `datacenter.hostFolder` it works as expected.

So the fact that vxpd crashes when doing this seems to be a defect, and possibly with security implications.

issue_title: Sqlite not supported?
Custom module import behavior (support for Django)
issue_body:
Running monkeytype against a file with Django models or dependencies on Django apps gives me a series of errors.

First, just `monkeytype apply package.app.models`:

```
django.core.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured: Requested setting DEFAULT_INDEX_TABLESPACE, but settings are not configured. You must either define the environment variable DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE or call settings.configure() before accessing settings.
```

Second, `DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=config.settings.local monkeytype apply package.app.models`:

```
File "package/app/models.py"
django.core.exceptions.AppRegistryNotReady: Apps aren't loaded yet.
```

Third, adding

```python
import django
django.setup()
```

to the top of package.app.models:

```
File "some/other/models.py"
django.core.exceptions.AppRegistryNotReady: Apps aren't loaded yet.
```

What **has** worked for me is patching `monkeytype/cli.py` and `monkeytype/util.py` to add

```python
+   import django
+   django.setup()
    mod = importlib.import_module(module)
```

Obviously this isn't suitable as-is because it breaks monkeytype on non-Django codebases. However, it did give me the idea to have an `ModuleImporter` (name WIP) context manager that returns `importlib.import_module(module)` but may also perform pre-/post-import actions. It would work similarly to TypeRewriter: the default implementation could just `import_module`, but a DjangoModuleImporter could call `django.setup()`, and the specific ModuleImporter could be configured in settings.

Thoughts? Happy to submit a PR for this if you think it's the right direction.

## Summary

Those processes are destroyed when their parent dies. I use Minio in Celery task and the Celery worker is always running. Having those unterminated processes causes a huge memory leak (approximately the size of the uploaded file).

```
Python 3.5.3
minio == 3.0.0.
```

## To reproduce
Put a large file named `payload1.apk` in the same directory as the following script.
```
from minio import Minio

client = Minio('localhost:9000',

issue_title: Improve permanance initialization scheme for spatial pooler
issue_body:
@subutai 

In the spatial pooler, synaptic permanence should be initialized such that a small number of learning steps could make it disconnected or connected. This idea is described in the doc https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/src/nupic/research/spatial_pooler.py#L1130

However, the function _initPermConnected and _initPermNonConnected does not achieve what is described in the doc. Connected permanence values are uniformly distributed between the connected threshold and the maximum permanence. With the default values of synPermConnected=0.1, synPermMax=1.0, many connected synapses were well above the connected threshold. During learning, it could take hundred of learning steps to get these connected synapses below threshold (see experiment results here https://github.com/numenta/nupic.research/pull/586). 

I suggest to initialize the permanence values differently. Instead of a uniform distribution, use a Gaussian distribution centered around the connected threshold, with a standard deviation proportional to synPermActiveInc and synPermInactiveDec respectively. This will ensure that a small number of steps can make a synapse connected/disconnected.

issue_title: Improve usage of regionLevel and requireSplitterMap
issue_body:
We should remove regionLevel and requireSplitterMap from Spec.hpp/cpp and PyRegion.cpp. These flags are obsolete and make it harder to understand region spec's. For backward compatibility, we should still allow them until they are completely removed from NuPIC, but ignore them.

Alternatively, as a simpler first step, in PyRegion.cpp, make the Spec items regionLevel and requireSplitterMap optional. regionLevel should default to `true` and requireSplitterMap should default to `false`

issue_title: On resume crash in SDL2 bootstrap
issue_body:
This problem actually was noted by another reviewer in ticket #646, but I suspect they stopped seeing it because only certain situations seem to trigger it. Specifically, it appears that rotation and pause / resume can cause the PythonActivity to be destroyed and re-created, even if the app is still running in the background. The behavior is documented in the Android docs here:

https://developer.android.com/training/basics/activity-lifecycle/recreating.html

To avoid having a complete app restart, it appears that Android caches the app state using the Bundle instance and tries to restore the app state automatically on resume. This is why onCreate gets a Bundle object that sometimes is empty (new start), and sometimes is not (rotation change or resume).

Unfortunately, it appears the state saving behavior is "under the hood" and it would take a deep look into Android's code to know what state it saves and when. It does seem to differ from app to app, which is why the problem is not persistent and easily reproduced. 

When it does happen, you get the crash noted in #646, and tracking that down, you find that the problem is that when onCreate is called with a non-empty Bundle, it causes mLayout and mImageView to be restored from cache, so the attempt in showLoadingScreen to add mImageView to mLayout gives a 'duplicate view' error. 

I'm going to submit a pull request with a fix for this. I didn't discover a way to check if a view exists, and I wasn't sure if doing a try block with removeView would have any side-effects, so I went with the solution of only setting mImageView to mLayout if we don't have an existing mImageView. The potential side-effect is that resume may not show the splash screen, but resume startup won't have the potential unzip slowdown, and the Bundle resuming should reduce the loading time on resume at least somewhat. 

Anyway, I'm open to a different or better approach if anyone knows of one. :) 

Also, I think #730 and #732 are affected by recreating the PythonActivity based on their problem descriptions, but I do not know this for certain.

issue_title: Timeout, verify_ssl options not supported by gevent transport
issue_body:
The docs for transport state at the beginning "All transports should support at least the following options" which is then followed by a list of timeout, verify_ssl and ca_certs.

But when trying to use the gevent+http transport, I get an error: `__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'timeout'` from `raven.transport.TransportRegistry.get_transport` on the line `self._transports[full_url] = self._schemes[parsed_url.scheme](parsed_url, **options)`

Looking at `raven.transport.gevent.GeventedHTTPTransport` its `__init__` indeed doesn't take these three arguments, but instead just the special `maximum_outstanding_requests` argument.

I think that init method should be changed to also take the three arguments, then pass these through to the super call, which ends up calling the `HTTPTransport.__init__` which indeed does take these arguments.

issue_title: Broken top_level.txt file in distribution
issue_body:
This is what the [setuptools documentation](https://pythonhosted.org/setuptools/formats.html#top-level-txt-conflict-management-metadata) says about the `top_level.txt`file:

> This file is a list of the top-level module or package names provided by the project, one Python identifier per line.
>
> Subpackages are not included; a project containing both a ``foo.bar`` and a ``foo.baz`` would include only one line, ``foo``, in its ``top_level.txt``.

The file distributed with rdflib can be accessed with:

    >>> import pkg_resources
    >>> provider = pkg_resources.get_distribution('rdflib')
    >>> print(provider.get_metadata('top_level.txt'))
    rdflib
    rdflib/extras
    rdflib/plugins
    rdflib/plugins/parsers
    rdflib/plugins/parsers/pyMicrodata
    rdflib/plugins/parsers/pyRdfa
    rdflib/plugins/parsers/pyRdfa/extras
    rdflib/plugins/parsers/pyRdfa/host
    rdflib/plugins/parsers/pyRdfa/rdfs
    rdflib/plugins/parsers/pyRdfa/transform
    rdflib/plugins/serializers
    rdflib/plugins/sparql
    rdflib/plugins/sparql/results
    rdflib/plugins/stores
    rdflib/tools

and is clearly broken as it should only contain le first line.

I think the problem is that the `packages` variable in `setup.py` is not what should be passed to the `packages=` keyword argument.

issue_title: Manipulating WCS and CelestialCoordinates to place a location on a WFIRST SCA
issue_body:
Having experimented with `demo13.py` and knowing a bit about the WFIRST coordinate systems, I thought I would try to simulate a piece of real sky that I have a catalog for. However, I'm not sure how to place the location I want on a particular SCA.

Supplying the coordinate I want as the `world_pos=` argument to `wfirst.getWCS()` places the target in the center of the focal plane array (so, not on any of the SCAs). I tried to work out the correct direction and angle to offset the coordinate using the stuff in `galsim.wfirst.wfirst_wcs` to no avail.

I'm also having trouble understanding the plot I get from this code to show the SCA centers as projected on the sky. It looks like there may be a parity flip somewhere (comparing to this plot: https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/wiki/GalSim-WFIRST-module-diagrams).

![wfirst_sca_galsim](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/172886/28999828-11833062-7a25-11e7-9e73-932669240833.png)

```
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt
import galsim
from galsim import wfirst
from galsim.wfirst import wfirst_wcs

import datetime
vernal_equinox_2025 = datetime.datetime(2025,3,20,9,2,0)
subframe_ra, subframe_dec = (11.721605694716114, 85.277622661476954)
targ_pos = galsim.CelestialCoord(
    ra=subframe_ra * galsim.degrees,
    dec=subframe_dec * galsim.degrees,
)
wcs_lookup = wfirst.getWCS(world_pos=targ_pos, date=vernal_equinox_2025)
sca_ra_centers, sca_dec_centers = [], []
for i in range(1, 19):
    center = wcs_lookup[i].toWorld(galsim.PositionD(wfirst.n_pix/2, wfirst.n_pix/2))
    ra, dec = center.ra / galsim.degrees, center.dec / galsim.degrees
    plt.scatter(ra, dec)
    plt.text(ra, dec, str(i))
plt.scatter(subframe_ra, subframe_dec)
```

issue_title: SyntaxError when parsing per-argument type comments with comma on the next line
issue_body:
In the PR adding per-argument type comment support https://github.com/python/typed_ast/pull/5 there is an unhandled case when comma for an argument is after the type comment, therefore the current version of `typed_ast` errors out on the following.

```
In [1]: import inspect

In [2]: import typed_ast.ast3

In [3]: def lovely(spam  # type: bool
   ...:         , eggs  # type: int
   ...:         , ham  # type: str
   ...:         ):
   ...:     # type: (...) -> bool
   ...:     return spam
   ...: 

In [4]: typed_ast.ast3.parse(inspect.getsource(lovely))
  File "<unknown>", line 2
    , eggs  # type: int
    ^
SyntaxError: invalid syntax
```

Are there any plans to support this case?

```
In [5]: import ast

In [6]: ast.parse(inspect.getsource(lovely))
Out[6]: <_ast.Module at 0x7fb9584b84e0>
```

The built-in `ast` succeeds, which seems to be in conflict with the [development philosophy](https://github.com/python/typed_ast#development-philosophy) of `typed_ast`.

> This project is a drop-in replacement for the builtin ast module. It is intended to be bug-for-bug compatible and behave identically, except for the presence of a few additional fields on the returned classes and a few additional optional arguments to the parse call.

issue_title: Patroni does not perform failover if master lost data directory
issue_body:
We are migrating from 1.2 to 1.3.4 and one of our tests fails - we removed data directory and are expecting failover to replica and master reinitialization. But with new version master detects empty dir, releases key and .. wait for leader to bootstrap. Meanwhile replica is considering master as "master" and wait for master too. 
From master: 

> 2017-10-11 11:35:27,431 ERROR: get_postgresql_status
> Traceback (most recent call last):
>   File "/patroni/patroni/api.py", line 405, in get_postgresql_status
>     retry=retry)[0]
>   File "/patroni/patroni/api.py", line 381, in query
>     return retry(self.server.query, sql, *params)
>   File "/patroni/patroni/utils.py", line 259, in __call__
>     return func(*args, **kwargs)
>   File "/patroni/patroni/api.py", line 452, in query
>     raise e
> OperationalError: could not open file "base/12404/2600": No such file or directory
> 
> 2017-10-11 11:35:27,433 INFO: released leader key voluntarily as data dir empty and currently leader
> ...
> 2017-10-11 11:35:37,416 INFO: Lock owner: None; I am node1
> 2017-10-11 11:35:37,420 INFO: waiting for leader to bootstrap

From slave:

> 2017-10-11 14:20:50,223 INFO: Got response from node1 http://10.129.1.63:8008/patroni: {"state": "unknown", "database_system_identifier": "6475561736777527351", "role": "master", "patroni": {"scope": "common", "version": "1.3.4"}}
> 2017-10-11 14:20:50,262 WARNING: Master (node1) is still alive
> 2017-10-11 14:20:50,267 INFO: following a different leader because i am not the healthiest node

I suppose that replica cannot promote itself because broken master declares wrong status. Broken master declares wrong status because it released key but never update `postgresql.role` value.

issue_title: Auth API based client
issue_body:
First, some context :_)

I'm going to implement CartoDB/cartoframes/issues/170, which will provide easy access to example datasets and maps from CARTO Frames. Those examples will be loaded from a CARTO account with example maps and datasets.

CARTO Frames always checks [if the provided user and API key are valid for authentication](https://github.com/CartoDB/cartoframes/blob/4355e19157bd59e64979901788edfac3458bf1e7/cartoframes/context.py#L117), and that's a problem for a test account. Unless we dropped the requirement for an API key (and that's something that we all want to avoid) we must use the soon-to-be-released Auth API default public API key. Although Auth API is not feature-complete yet, today we've released an improvement that allows validating any pair of user and API key: CartoDB/cartodb/pull/13564.

Currently, [`APIKeyAuthClient`](https://github.com/CartoDB/carto-python/blob/85093cf859252a374381a3ab92ca2d676d9338a6/carto/auth.py#L29) is based on `api_key` parameter, and, as the migration to Auth API will be gradual, that shouldn't change soon.

As I need a client to access CARTO based on Auth API _header_ to check validation, I propose implementing a `AuthAPIKeyClient` that extends `APIKeyAuthClient`. Its responsability is the same than `APIKeyAuthClient` (providing an authenticated way to send requests to CARTO), but using the header instead of the parameter.

Then, I'd move the [`is_authenticated`](https://github.com/CartoDB/cartoframes/blob/4355e19157bd59e64979901788edfac3458bf1e7/cartoframes/context.py#L117) method from CARTO Frames to carto-python, because it seems useful.

I'd like to create a new class, `AuthAPIKeyClient`, because currently, [`send`](https://github.com/CartoDB/carto-python/blob/85093cf859252a374381a3ab92ca2d676d9338a6/carto/auth.py#L66) doesn't have a way to send headers. In order to avoid creating a new class, we should add a "use auth header flag" somewhere and infest the code with ifs, which doesn't seem too clean.

Please keep in mind that I've just begun programming Python, so all of this might be bullshit :-) I wanted to share my approach before beginning coding.

What do you think, @andy-esch @alrocar @danicarrion ?

PS: the implementation of `is_authenticated` will be based in the improved Auth API endpoint (CartoDB/cartodb/pull/13564), removing [the query to `information_schema.tables`](https://github.com/CartoDB/cartoframes/blob/4355e19157bd59e64979901788edfac3458bf1e7/cartoframes/context.py#L120-L121).

issue_title: None of the deserializers work when using the 'multi:softprob' objective with Sagemaker XGBoost
issue_body:
### System Information
- **Spark or PySpark**: PySpark
- **SDK Version**: Bundled version that comes with EMR 5.13.0
- **Spark Version**: Bundled version that comes with EMR 5.13.0
- **Algorithm**: XGBoost (Classifier)

### Describe the problem
When using the 'multi:softprob' objective with Sagemaker the end point returns an array of doubles with the probability for each class. However, the default deserializer for XGBoost (XGBoostCSVRowDeserializer) tries to interpret the result as a Double when it tries to construct the dataframe in PySpark. This obviously fails with a NumberFormatException. Here is a stack trace.

```

  File "/usr/lib/spark/python/lib/py4j-0.10.6-src.zip/py4j/protocol.py", line 320, in get_return_value

: org.apache.spark.SparkException: Job aborted due to stage failure: Task 0 in stage 21.0 failed 1 times, most recent failure: Lost task 0.0 in stage 21.0 (TID 419, localhost, executor driver): java.lang.NumberFormatException: For input string: "[0.9360387325286865"
	at sun.misc.FloatingDecimal.readJavaFormatString(FloatingDecimal.java:2043)
	at sun.misc.FloatingDecimal.parseDouble(FloatingDecimal.java:110)
	at java.lang.Double.parseDouble(Double.java:538)
	at scala.collection.immutable.StringLike$class.toDouble(StringLike.scala:284)
	at scala.collection.immutable.StringOps.toDouble(StringOps.scala:29)
	at com.amazonaws.services.sagemaker.sparksdk.transformation.deserializers.XGBoostCSVRowDeserializer$$anonfun$deserializeResponse$1.apply(XGBoostCSVRowDeserializer.scala:41)
	at com.amazonaws.services.sagemaker.sparksdk.transformation.deserializers.XGBoostCSVRowDeserializer$$anonfun$deserializeResponse$1.apply(XGBoostCSVRowDeserializer.scala:41)
```

### Minimal repo / logs
Build an XGBoost estimator with the objective as 'multi:softprob'. Say,
```python
    estimator = XGBoostSageMakerEstimator(
        trainingInstanceType=args.instance_type,
        trainingInstanceCount=args.instance_count,
        endpointInstanceType="ml.m4.xlarge",
        endpointInitialInstanceCount=1,
        sagemakerRole=IAMRole(SAGEMAKER_ROLE)
    )
    estimator.setObjective('multi:softprob')
    estimator.setNumClasses(2)
    estimator.setNumRound(10)
```
This successfully builds the endpoint. However, when you try to predict using the endpoint that was built above it fails with the stack trace pasted above
```python
    xgboost_model = SageMakerModel(
        endpointInstanceType=None,
        endpointInitialInstanceCount=None,
        requestRowSerializer=LibSVMRequestRowSerializer(),
        responseRowDeserializer=XGBoostCSVRowDeserializer(),

        endpointCreationPolicy=EndpointCreationPolicy.DO_NOT_CREATE,
        prependResultRows=False
    )

    transformed_data = xgboost_model.transform(df)
```

- **Exact command to reproduce**:
Read above. The endpoint is correctly able to predict when I set the objective as 'multi:softmax'. However, the deserializer just fails when the objective is 'multi:softprob'. I tried using `LibSVMResponseRowDeserializer` for giggles since I trained it with libsvm data, and as expected it didn't work either.
None of the deserializers listed here seem to be relevant either
```python
from .deserializers import (
    ResponseRowDeserializer,
    XGBoostCSVRowDeserializer, ProtobufResponseRowDeserializer,
    KMeansProtobufResponseRowDeserializer, PCAProtobufResponseRowDeserializer,

issue_title: Include ethereum API in manticore package

issue_title: Default principal: `TypeError: argument 2 must be string, not None`
issue_body:
Python: 2.7.2
requests: 2.10.0
requests-kerberos: 0.9.0
kerberos: 1.2.4

    ...
      File "<>/common/runtime/session.py", line 38, in create
        auth=requests_kerberos.HTTPKerberosAuth())
      File "<>/requests/sessions.py", line 487, in get
        return self.request('GET', url, **kwargs)
      File "<>/requests/sessions.py", line 475, in request
        resp = self.send(prep, **send_kwargs)
      File "<>/requests/sessions.py", line 591, in send
        r = dispatch_hook('response', hooks, r, **kwargs)
      File "<>/requests/hooks.py", line 31, in dispatch_hook
        _hook_data = hook(hook_data, **kwargs)
      File "<>/requests_kerberos/kerberos_.py", line 281, in handle_response
        _r = self.handle_401(response, **kwargs)
      File "<>/requests_kerberos/kerberos_.py", line 189, in handle_401
        _r = self.authenticate_user(response, **kwargs)
      File "<>/requests_kerberos/kerberos_.py", line 164, in authenticate_user
        auth_header = self.generate_request_header(response, host)
      File "<>/requests_kerberos/kerberos_.py", line 122, in generate_request_header
        gssflags=gssflags, principal=self.principal)

This appears to be related to the "Explicit Principal" change. I ran into it after replacing v0.8.0 with v0.9.0, and can reproduce the same behaviour in v0.10.0. Passing the principal explicitly to `HTTPKerberosAuth`'s initialiser fixes the issue, as does replacing `kwargs['principal'] = self.principal` on line 128 of `kerberos_.py` with `pass` in v0.10.0.

issue_title: Executing only one pubsub callback concurrently
issue_body:
I have a problem, where I depend on stateful externals resources, that require me to only process one message at a time.

I was trying to accomplish that by setting the max_messages in the FlowControl to 1. However, the callbacks were still executed concurrently.

So I dug a bit deeper and found [this commit](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/commit/76a0f5a3aab1723d35a2d31b13547bc570da1948#commitcomment-26621784). I thought I could pass an event and use that to block the thread until the execution of the callback is done. 

However, it was not entirely clear to me how this is meant to be use. The only place I found, where an event can be passed is the constructor of the future. However, the docstring discourages to use this class.

So, is the intended way to use this, to implement a new policy? Because this is the place, where I would have assumed the event to be passed through.

If I'm completely off track and if there's some completely different way of going about this, please let me know. 

I appreciate you work!

issue_title: BigQuery should have a module that follows the DB-API to allow for a SQLAlchemy dialect
issue_labels: [{'id': 82126549, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20bigquery', 'name': 'api: bigquery', 'color': 'c0c0c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2+', 'name': 'priority: p2+', 'color': 'ffee58', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438582, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20enhancement', 'name': 'type: enhancement', 'color': '8bc34a', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
There is a module to connect [SQLAlchemy to BigQuery, but it only has partial support](https://github.com/cpdean/sqlalchemy-bigquery/issues/6). I'd love to be able to connect to BigQuery using SQLAlchemy this way and be able to play with [Caravel](https://github.com/airbnb/caravel) as was mentioned in that partial support issue.

To do this properly, we'd need to provide a module with an [implementation of a SQLAlchemy dialect](http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/core/internals.html#sqlalchemy.engine.default.DefaultDialect) I would imagine we'd want to package this separately so as not to add a dependency on SQLAlchemy. Looking more closely at the [dialect interface](http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/core/internals.html#sqlalchemy.engine.interfaces.Dialect) it looks like the database driver would need to implement the [Python DB-API](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0249/).

I'm considering this a low priority feature request, but do think it would be cool.

issue_body:
Folks,

  I have an issue with happbase/bigtable client usage in GCE. I have reproed the same in the following script - could one of you please take a look? This is blocking our fleet from talking to BigTable. 

  FWIW the script runs fine as far as the scan from current thread (The base table has two rows that match if it matters). Right afterwards when I try to run it from a background thread (pool) using map, this hangs for ever.

import os

from multiprocessing import Pool

print results

print "SUBU: Trying pool - HANGS after this"
threads = Pool(4)
threadResults = threads.map(_scan_table, table_config)
threads.close()
threads.join()
print threadResults

issue_title: Comment out codes in _bootstrap-variables.scss are written in sass syntax(missing semicolon).
eyeglass import is not consistent with rails/bower/node/etc
issue_body:
In [#1007](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass/pull/1007), Eyeglass support was added. This was a great improvement for us as it allowed us to pull more dependencies in through npm.

We have a sass library that works both in Rails's asset pipeline and in other ecosystems. The eyeglass import having a different name in Eyeglass unfortunately makes us choose between only supporting building through eyeglass, or not having eyeglass support and having everything else work.

For example, in Rails:

``` scss
@import "bootstrap";
```

But in eyeglass:

``` scss
@import "bootstrap-sass/bootstrap";
```

If you look at the README in its current state you can see how consistent it is until you get down to the Eyeglass section.

This could be fixed by adding a `name` field to the `eyeglass` section package.json for `bootstrap-sass`:

``` json
{
  "name": "bootstrap-sass",
  "version": "1.1.0",
  "eyeglass": {
    "name": "bootstrap",
    "needs": "^0.6.7",
    "exports": "eyeglass-exports.js"
  }
```

For consistency, I think it would be great if you could `@import "bootstrap"` in any ecosystem and have it work.

This would be a breaking change for anybody currently using eyeglass, but an easy fix to implement (change your import statements to "@import bootstrap").

issue_title: make `Target` cacheable in the `ProductGraph`
issue_body:
currently, `BuildGraph` construction is responsible for the ~majority of our runtime in the warm case. much of the cost here lies in `Target` instantiation which happens on each run by way of converting cached `LegacyTarget`/`TargetAdaptor` objects.

if we can instead construct `Target` objects directly in the daemon context and thus make them cacheable in the resident `ProductGraph`, then we can theoretically skirt their construction costs on warm runs and simply link them together within a per-run`BuildGraph`.

in order to do this, we'll probably have to:

1) kill all mutable state on Target
2) remove `Target`->`BuildGraph` linkages
3) <insert other things here after investigation>

                         Writing class dependency file to /Users/zundel/Src/pants/.pants.d/compile/jvm/java/jmake-depfiles/global_depfile
                         Checking org/pantsbuild/example/distance/Distances
                         Recompiling source files:
                         /Users/zundel/Src/pants/.pants.d/gen/protoc/gen-java/org/pantsbuild/example/distance/Foo.java
                         Reading existing dependency file at /Users/zundel/Src/pants/.pants.d/compile/jvm/java/jmake-depfiles/global_depfile
                         Writing class dependency file to /Users/zundel/Src/pants/.pants.d/compile/jvm/java/jmake-depfiles/global_depfile
                         Writing project database...  Done.
                         
FAILURE: compilation error


               Waiting for background workers to finish.
15:21:56 00:01   [complete]
               FAILURE
```

What's happening here is that the `message Foo` definition generates a new file `.pants.d/gen/protoc/gen-java/org/pantsbuild/example/distance/Foo.java`.  When you remove the definition there is nothing in pants to clean up the generated file, but some of the symbols it refers to in another file `Distances.java` are removed when that file is regenerated.

One workaround is to run `./pants clean-all`
Another workaround is to remove `option java_multiple_files = true` (but this will change the generated API)

issue_title: New engine throws exception when running bundle command
issue_body:
JvmApp.bundles is type BundleField. In v1 engine, BundleField elements are of type BundleProps, but in v2 engine, BundleField elements are of type BundleAdaptor.

When computing fingerprint for BundleField, the helper function makes the assumption that BundleProps is the element type, thus it tries to access fields which only exist in BundleProps but not in BundleAdaptor. This triggers the exception.
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/targets/jvm_app.py#L170-182

To reproduce, run this command:
./pants --enable-v2-engine bundle testprojects/src/java/org/pantsbuild/testproject/bundle:bundle

issue_title: Make it possible to remove duplicate sequences based on read id
issue_body: The `--removeDuplicates` argument to `filter-fasta.py` only uses the read sequences to determine what is a duplicate. Using it can result in removing multiple distinct reads, which may not be what you want. Add a `--removeDuplicatesById` option to do the removing based just on read id. This is useful for cases where a set of reads is matched against a database using multiple methods and as a final step you need to remove duplicates based just on id.

issue_title: Physical interface monitoring support?
issue_body:
Hi Thomas,

Is there any support within the ExaBGP code-base for physical interface monitoring? I mean a way to shutdown a directly-connected peer BGP session if the corresponding physical interface goes down (like during a physical fiber-cut or switch/port maintenance), without waiting for the BGP session expiration timer or resorting to short TCP keepalive tricks?

It would be quite beneficial in HA/CLOS scenarii, where all servers maintain a double BGP session towards 2 different switches, and one of the switches crashes (or the attachment port is administratively shut): we would like to remove one of the 2 ECMP outgoing routes as quickly as possible, hence being notified of a "peer down" situation almost instantaneously in this case.

Thanks for your answer and sorry if this is something that's already been asked in the past.

Pierre-Yves

issue_title: Wrong default arguments for docker build
issue_body:
When running `docker build`, it (by default) removes intermediate containers if they succeeded, otherwise it leaves them behind. This is achieved by Docker internally setting `--rm=true` when building. You can verify this [here](https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/build/).

The issue with `docker-py` is that it defaults to `--rm=false`, meaning it always leaves the intermediate containers, which, in addition to not being default behavior, is highly annoying to have to cleanup, especially when using `docker-py` with third party software, where there's no direct control over `docker-py`.

The fix is to simply set `rm=False` to `rm=True` here:
https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/master/docker/api/build.py#L17
