issue_title: Read XMP data from a BytesIO buffer (not a physical file)
issue_body:
I'd like to use the XMP Toolkit on a web server. The way the framework is set up the image storage is abstracted from the actual file. There might not even be a file at all. It is passed around the app as a `os.BytesIO` object.

Unfortunately the XMP Toolkit API only accepts file paths, and not file pointers. It looks like this is a limitation of the underlying Exempi library since all calls to the library pass the file name.

Is there maybe another way to use a BytesIO object or a Bytestring directly?

issue_title: Unexpected processing order of received messages
issue_body:
First of all: I guess we have no bug here, but a behavior which might need some documentation (if not existing yet).

Consider the following case:
A client subscribes to a topic and calls a function. The result of the function is awaited.

Now the server first publishes something on the topic and instantly afterward returns the result of the function call.

What happens now on the client side is that first the subscription handler is fired (synchronously I guess) and then once the future is resolved the function call result is processed.

The behavior can be easily simulated:
#### Client:
```
async def onJoin(self, details):
    await self.subscribe(self.handle_change, 'changed')

    all_changes_future = self.call('all_changes')
    await self.call('make_change', 'a change')

    all_changes = await all_changes_future

    print('all_changes: ', all_changes)

def handle_change(self, change):
    logging.info('change# %s', change)
```

#### Server
```
@wamp.register('make_change')
def make_change(self, the_change):
    print('make_change')
    self.all_changes.append(the_change)

    self.publish('changed', the_change)

    return the_change

@wamp.register('all_changes')
def all_changes(self):
    print('all_changes')
    return self.all_changes
```

Complete example code can be found here: https://gist.github.com/aberres/0c5b356190991b1d0204bbf5ca399e05

The server will print
> all_changes
> make_change

while we see
> change:  a change
> all_changes:  ['a change', 'a change']

on the client side.

I'd say technically this behavior is correct. It might be unexpected for a developer though.

When could this be a problem?
I noticed this in practice when I have a function to retrieve the current state of some objects and send around any changes as delta.
Now there is a possibility for a race the moment the client registers.
The complete state might not have been handled yet while a subscription is already processed.

I can solve this problem by adding sequence numbers to all sent packages. I can then process the received deltas with a higher sequence number once the complete state is read.

issue_title: Link_df runs very slow with large data sets
issue_body:
I'm attempting to link particle positions to form trajectories using link_df for a data set containing ~1,700 particles for 90,000 frames.  In attempting this, I noticed link_df was running very slow.  After 12 hours, the code had completed under 8,000 frames (I estimate that the entire data set would have taken maybe 4 days).  While I understand that that is a huge data set, I previously was able to complete the linking of the same number of particles (~1,700) for 24,000 frames, which took about 4 hours.  Naively, I was expecting the time to scale linearly, and so this 90k frame data set would take 12-16 hours (much more reasonable than 4 days).  But this was not the case.

My hypothesis is there is a memory issue.  The 90k frame data file is ~3 GB, and uses 10-15% of my machine's memory (linux server, found using "top" command).  Any suggestions on how this process can be sped up?

Some useful information, versions of various packages are: python 2.7.10, numba 0.12.2, numpy 1.8.2, trackpy 0.2.4rc7, and pandas 0.14.1.

Thanks for your help.

issue_title: raise validation error if trying to send ether to a contract function with payable=False
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

When methods are marked with `payable=False` in the ABI, a transaction sending ether to them will (or should) fail. Web3 isn't doing anything to intercept these calls, leaving callers to debug the failing transaction.

### How can it be fixed?

When running a call, transact, estimateGas, or buildTransaction, inspect the transaction's `value` field. If it is present, and non-0, check the function ABI. If payable is present and `False`, raise a validation error explaining that the user must not send ether to functions that are not payable.

Alternative approach: when searching for matching functions in the ABI, filter out options that are not payable if the transaction has non-0 value. Not sure that this really buys anything besides fitting into a framework we already have.

issue_title: explicit() doesn't remove existing package first => try_hard_link() fails => symlinks are used => relative RPATHs in executables do not work.
issue_body:
I was looking into why symlinks were being used everywhere as it was preventing me from testing packages installed with a full path.

It seems that `explicit()` (or something in that callstack) doesn't remove the existing package first.

This means that `try_hard_link()` will fail because `dst` exists and `assert not isfile(dst), dst` will fire. This gets caught in `explicit()` which (I guess) makes the assumption that hardlinks are not possible.

Conda then goes off and symlinks everything which causes `RPATH` to fail in executables as symlinks are followed before the RPATH is canonicalized/normalized.

I'm not sure if there's a reason not to unlink first? But this bug also gives us the opportunity to mark executables as such and *never* symlink them.

> ```

> Loading channels: done
> # Name                  Version           Build  Channel
> copy-jdk-configs-cos7-ppc64le             3.3               0  pkgs/main
> java-1.7.0-openjdk-cos6-i686       1.7.0.131      h3a8c334_0  pkgs/main

> java-1.7.0-openjdk-devel-cos7-ppc64le       1.7.0.171               0  pkgs/main
> java-1.7.0-openjdk-headless-cos7-ppc64le       1.7.0.171               0  pkgs/main
> openjdk                 8.0.121               0  pkgs/free

> openjdk                 8.0.152      h393ad39_1  pkgs/main
> openjdk                 8.0.152      he2ab291_0  pkgs/main
> ```

@msarahan 
> right, but I'd bet most people do: `conda search jdk`

> ```
> $ conda search jdk
> Loading channels: done
> 
> PackagesNotFoundError: The following packages are not available from current channels:
> 
>   - jdk
> 
> Current channels:
> 
>   - https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r/osx-64
>   - https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r/noarch

> and use the search bar at the top of the page.

@kalefranz

> I see.  At a minimum, we should suggest using wildcards in the PackagesNotFound message for `conda search` (though not for `create`, `install`, `update`, `remove`).

issue_title: String interpolation of values from other sections
issue_body:
Hi, I'm just re-posting a feature request which was already issued [here]( https://code.google.com/p/configobj/issues/detail?id=18), and which I'd find very useful. 

It would be very nice if one could interpolate values from other sections. ConfigParser can do this: https://docs.python.org/3.4/library/configparser.html#configparser.ExtendedInterpolation

Example config file:

    [sec_a]
        a = a

    [sec_b]
        b = ${sec_a.a}b

    [sec_c]
        c = ${sec_b.b}c

and then in the ConfigObj config one would get

    config['sec_c']['c'] == 'abc'

I don't care about the exact syntax.
Is that somehow possible with configobj now or is it possible to add this in a future release?
Thanks

issue_title: [Test] Run test cases in parallel to improve build efficiency.
issue_body:

All of our tests are VCR.py based. That means the test executions are already encapsulated enough.

I'd like to explorer the possibility of running tests in parallel in order to shorten the build time on CI.

issue_title: [dev_setup.py] In Windows, `check_call(command, shell=True)` breaks when users has AutoRun command in registry
issue_body:
As stated in title, [the dev_setup.py script](https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/blob/master/scripts/dev_setup.py) will break in Windows.
When user has AutoRun command in Registry, below error message is thrown.
`Couldn't open requirements file: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'requirements.txt'`
Root cause is [here](https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20071121-00/?p=24433).

A simple fix is to remove the "shell=true" parameter. But I am not sure whether this flag is required in Linux. Haven't tested it yet.
So maybe we can just fix this for Windows.

issue_title: Sparse Transitions
issue_body:
It would be neat if this library was able to optimize sparse transitions (e.g. if there are O(# states) transitions) - currently Viterbi takes O((#states)^2) time regardless of whether a number of transitions are invalid. 

I'm not too familiar with Cython, so I'm not sure if it's possible to simply swap out the transition matrix with a sparse representation like CSR and be able to loop through only the non-zero entries in a way that also works for non-sparse matrices (i.e. being able to use the same code for sparse and non-sparse matrices). If this is possible, I can take a shot at implementing it.

issue_title: ClientPayloadError with blank Content-Encoding header
issue_body:
In case response contains blank `Content-Encoding` header call to `await resp.text()` will cause an error:
```
aiohttp.client_exceptions.ClientPayloadError: 400, message='Can not decode content-encoding: '
```
To reproduce you may use this two scripts - server:
```
from aiohttp import web
from multidict import MultiDict


async def index(request):
    return web.Response(
        text='Hello Aiohttp!',
        headers=MultiDict({'CONTENT-ENCODING': ''}))

app = web.Application()
app.router.add_get('/', index)
web.run_app(app, host='127.0.0.1', port=8080)
```
and client:
```
import asyncio

import aiohttp


async def fetch_page():
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        async with session.get('http://127.0.0.1:8080/') as resp:
            for key, value in resp.headers.items():
                print("%s:" % key, value)
            print(resp.status)
            # Failed on this call
            print(await resp.text())


loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
loop.run_until_complete(fetch_page())
```
I don't know exactly why such thing happen, but I'd like to note that on versions of aiohttp prior 2.0 there is no such behavior. Other clients - wget/flask work without any errors.
And also seems that issue #1918 can have the same origin, but there `Content-Encoding` was provided as `deflate`.

Env:
Python 3.6.1
aiohttp==2.1.0
async-timeout==1.2.1
chardet==3.0.3
multidict==2.1.6
yarl==0.10.2

issue_title: prepared URL API
issue_body:
Is there a way to get prepared URL, which will be used in a query? Something like:
```
req = ClientSession().Request(method='GET', url='http://path.org', data={'a':1, 'b':2})
url = req.prepared_url() -> 'http://path.org?a=1&b=2'
req.headers.update({'auth': auth_fun(url)})
```
I need this for authentication purposes.
Till now I used `requests.Request.prepare().path_url` for the URL and yet sent aiohttp request, but I just found out that sometimes the argument ordering in the output URL will differ between the libraries. Nasty bug.

My first reflex was to try with `ClientRequest` but it requries YARL's URL object (I think?), which is doable I guess, but maybe there's something short and sweet already available.

Can you please help me out?

issue_title: Websocket reconnect counts toward kernel last_activity
issue_body:
When configuring the new idle kernel culling, I discovered that browser tabs left open that disconnect and reconnect to kernels count toward the `last_activity` time in the kernel API. This might be beneficial in some cases, but causes problems in shared resource environments. For example:

1. Laptop user opens a new notebook browser tab.
2. User creates a Spark session.
3. User forgets the tab is open.
4. User moves among different access points on campus, between home and work, closes and opens the laptop lid when leaving her desk, etc.
5. Every time the network reconnects, the websocket reconnects and the kernel activity API logs a fresh last idle time.
6. The kernel cull timeout is never effective until the user actually closes the tab, and so the shared Spark resources are not returned to the pool.

issue_title: Start a production subdomain registrar node
issue_body:
In order to ship subdomain support in the browser, we'd need to spin up a production subdomain registrar node that registers subdomains for a given domain.

We need to:

1. Figure out what domain to use.
2. Start a blockstack-core node which can submit updates on behalf of that domain.
3. Point a subdomain registrar at that blockstack-core node, and perform _some_ kind of spam protection.

For the spam protection, we could read the submitted zonefile, lookup the associated profile object, and require a valid social proof (which raises a related issue of whether or not social proofs will be valid with subdomain strings `foo.bar.id`).

Tagging @muneeb-ali @jcnelson for thoughts on spam control.

issue_title: API addition to get current key(s) for verifying ID-linked signatures
issue_body:
**What we're building:**

We are working on a service that dispatches a payload for signing to one or multiple users. These users then sign the payload with whatever key is associated with their blockchain-anchored ID. The service needs to verify the signed payload when it receives it back to ensure it is signed buy the user ID that was intended.

**What we need:**

We would like a simple API method that returns the latest public key(s) a user has associated with their ID.

issue_title: Fastq chunking takes too much RAM
issue_body:
Currently, the entire fastq chunk is being loaded into memory when it's being created. 
(done here:  https://github.com/BD2KGenomics/toil-vg/blob/master/src/toil_vg/vg_map.py#L117)

Given the size of fastq files, and the fact that biopython's internal representation is much bigger still, this is a major bottleneck.  Ex. A chunk for 1/3 of the 4G chr22 fastq takes about 20G of memory.  

This code needs to be written to stream input and output one record at a time.  It'll probably run faster too that way.  We may want to consider parallelizing eventually (but that can be a separate issue)

issue_body:
# Issue Title

"Could not copy" error when the project reference is shared between several projects

# General

Suppose that two projects of  the solution share one reference.

 <ProjectReference Include="..\........src\SomeSharedLibrary.csproj"  />

The first project  (the service) is running in console window with **dotnet run** , the other app is the client for this service and runs (_usually, but indeed does not matter_) from Visual Studio.

Whenever you stop the client project and run it again it will fail to compile with annoying error

> Severity	Code	Description	Project	File	Line	Suppression State
> Error	MSB3021	Unable to copy file "D:\projects\....\bin\Debug\netcoreapp1.1\....dll" to "bin\Debug\netcoreapp2.0\X.....". The process cannot access the file 'bin\Debug\netcoreapp2.0\...dll' because it is being used by another process.	.....	C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2017\Community\MSBuild\15.0\Bin\Microsoft.Common.CurrentVersion.targets	4277	

It happens regardless if you have made any changes or not, moreover, even though you stop and restart the service in console windows - it does not help. The error is still occur. 

Formerly I was able at least  to restart the service to get rid of error, without closing the window, Now it became impossible, I have to close window, then reopen it and re-run **dotnet run**. 

Also, if I just  make changes to the service and therefore want to restart it (client is stopped but Visual Studio is open) , it also fails to compile because of locked files. Need to close the window and open again.

I even do not dream about the normal scenario - e.g when I make changes to the  client project (_not touching Shared library - it gets changed very seldom_),  and I am able to run it again without stopping the service.

Tried **PrivateAssets="All"** didn't help, shared files are still get locked.

Therefore I have to terminate the service, then close the window, then reopen the window and run it again, run the client, reattach to service process (if I need to debug) - just to run the client app again, even without any changes in it. Nightmare !

issue_title: Suggestion: Support for platform packaging/ilmerge type capability
issue_body:
# Issue  Suggestion: Support for ilmerge/libZ etc. functionality

# General

I am currently trying to package up some tools for use on Windows, OSX and Linux. And it is a real bear.

Writing platform independent code is one thing. To date there is no such thing as platform independent installation. OSX, Windows and Linux all have very different ideas about how code should be distributed. iOS and Android have more.

The ability to generate docker containers from a project is really welcome. It would be nice if there was a similar feature that allowed a developer sitting on a Windows box to cut platform native installers for all the major platforms in one compile. Like the Docker tools, the installers need not be anything fancy, people could always upgrade to something better and more full featured (Wix etc). But having a system that is supported out of the box would be a major advance.

The idea that installing programs should require modification to other parts of the computer is bogus.  One of the things I like most about .NET is that installing a program does not affect anything else on the machine. Shared libraries, dll hell, no thank you. 

The problem with the ,NET solution is that by default, programs are still delivered in IKEA flat pack format, a collection of .dll files in a directory. This is also bogus. Shared libraries should be for code that is likely to be shared.  And even then, there should always be the option of producing a stand alone executable that doesn't rely on anything outside the one file.

Apple has realized this already and deliver their applications as .ZIP files. There are tools like libZ that purport to do this for .NET but they are independent efforts and they don't work with .NET Core 2.0. Worse, the approach to signing is of the 'make it work' variety, not 'make it safe'. Everyone should be using named keys, not key files. There are far too many of those floating about on GitHub as it is.

The delivery team must have solved these problems already to deliver .NET Core. If the means of doing this was generalized just slightly and packaged up, everyone could use it.

issue_title: renew() stops for any unhandled exception
issue_body: If we get an unhandled exception during the renewal attempt, it doesn't crash the client but it does make `renew()` stop at that point. For example, try setting `standalone_supported_challenges` to an invalid value in a lineage's renewal configuration file, then running `renew()`. When it tries to renew that lineage, it will crash with an exception, and no subsequent lineages will be renewed. This is presumably the case with respect to every kind of unhandled exception that might occur for any reason.

from marshmallow import Schema, fields, validate


class ValidateClass(Schema):

class NestClass(Schema):

issue_title: Support/examples for using TuriCreate with pandas?
issue_body:
The example in the README shows how to use Turi Create with an SFrame. However, [SFrame has not had a release since July 2016](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/SFrame).

pandas is pretty much the de-facto DataFrame library in the Python data science ecosystem. It is under [active development](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/SFrame), has a [substantial community](https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/stargazers) (with [tens of thousands of StackOverflow questions](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/pandas)), and provides [tools for many common steps in data analysis](http://pandas.pydata.org/pandas-docs/stable/basics.html).

Please consider aligning with the general momentum in the Python data science community by providing examples of using pandas with Turi Create.

issue_title: Optimise 'updatePlayIcons' function
issue_body:
The ``updatePlayIcons `` function (https://github.com/pimusicbox/mopidy-musicbox-webclient/blob/88ff29db4c30bd4abfb248efad994b3da3123009/mopidy_musicbox_webclient/static/js/gui.js#L623-L649) does not scale well for very large tracklists.

It is responsible for inserting the 'playing' icon next to items in the various track lists to indicate which track is currently playing. The problem is that it loops over every item in the list every time the track changes, and this is made worse by the fact that we could have the track appearing in multiple lists (e.g. on the Queue, Playlists, Browse, and Search panes simultaneously). The performance degradation becomes noticeable if the tracklist contains more than a few hundred tracks.

I'm not quite sure how best to optimise this in a way that will ensure that MMW always stays in sync, especially considering that we may miss a Mopidy playback state change event every so often (e.g. if the connection to Mopidy is broken for whatever reason).

Request you to let me know, how I can have  String Theorem in Z3 4.4.1 version.

issue_title: ffi.cdef result parsing causes build failure on PyPy 5.8 virtualenv
issue_body:
## Environment
PyPy 5.8 on virtualenv
Using the latest commit `a87324f`

## Description
PyPy 5.8 uses different error message format for cffi.CDefError to Python 2.7.13, which causes build failure.

Python 2.7.13 error format example
```
cannot parse "x &= 0xFFFFFFFFLL;"
:34:6: before: &=
```

PyPy 5.8 error format example
```
cannot parse "Int r = (x == 0) ? ((Bool)0) : ((Bool)1);"
<cdef source string>:31:30: before: 0
```
```
<cdef source string>:32: unexpected <FuncDef>: this construct is valid C but not valid in cdef()
```

The second error format causes out of bound exception, because it has two `:` but only one line.
https://github.com/angr/pyvex/blob/a87324f88aa621436d4b8077b828b92ce51e08c2/make_ffi.py#L41-L42

    device_type = StringField('DeviceType')
    device_id = StringField('DeviceID')
    open_id = StringField('OpenID')
    op_type = IntegerField('OpType')`
第412行应为：
`event = 'device_unsubscribe_status'`

这样的错误没有发现，看来社区里用来做微信硬件的不多。

issue_title: Timeout in Initializing connection..  Odroid XU4 - Pixhawk Mini
issue_body:
Hello all, I would really appreciate some help.

I currently have my Odroid XU4 connected to my pixhawk mini via a FTDI usb to serial converter (as per the tutorial found here: http://ardupilot.org/dev/docs/odroid-via-mavlink.html)

Basically, serial connection from telem port to the converter to serial to USB converter to the Odroid. 

In QGroundControl I also changed the SYS_COMPANION parameter to enable the TELEM port at the companion computer link and set the baud rate to 57600. I then saved and rebooted the pixhawk.

I then power up my Odroid and plug in the serial converter to a USB port. 

Upon runnning

MANUAL> `

`import dronekit

At which point in the terminal where i ran the mavproxy.py command I see a few random letters and numbers pop up randomly . The terminal where I ran the connection script outputs:

`>>> Link timeout, no heartbeat in last 5 seconds

I also tried running the quick start example on the dronekit website:

to which I recieve the following error:

`Starting copter simulator (SITL)

Ready to boot.

sitl = dronekit_sitl.start_default()

issue_title: Reading 'unsupported' vehicle state attributes?
issue_body: There are numerous state attributes of the Pixhawk2.1/Cube (APM:Copter-3.5.5) that Mission Planner displays in its status page that aren't included in the list of [supported attributes using dronekit](http://python.dronekit.io/guide/vehicle_state_and_parameters.html).  As just one example, I would like to be able to read the 'press_abs' variable  (pressure reported by the internal barometer).  Is there any way to do this with the official release of dronekit?

issue_title: 扩展数据源
issue_body: 您好： 最近在学习使用rqalpha框架，**想使用自己定制的数据源**，按着http://rqalpha.readthedocs.io/zh_CN/latest/development/data_source.html#tushare中的内容： - 继承了BaseDataSource并override了对应接口，实现了自己数据源的抽象类； - 实现并加载了对应Mod； 按着http://rqalpha.readthedocs.io/zh_CN/latest/intro/tutorial.html#id3中的内容： - 写了个test策略做测试 其中 ![2018-05-14 6 56 39](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25770147/39993750-83591384-57a9-11e8-8bff-39f0465b7986.png) 将context.s1内容替换为了自己数据源中的symbol_code，e.g:000001.SZ，运行策略时报了下列错误 个人理解是000001.SZ这个股票代码不是预设好instrument类， 所以想请问我哪里理解有错误吗.. 如果想要扩展自有数据源，有哪些一定要改动的地方，或者有其他再详细一点教程可以参考吗，感谢了..

issue_title: Holes Filling doesn't works correctly with RealSense SDK v2.10.4
issue_body: | Required Info | | |---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- | | SDK Version | 2.10.4 | ### Issue Description This is a bug report. Holes filling of rs2::spatial_filter doesn't works correctly with RealSense SDK v2.10.4. The holes doesn't filled even if set option to filling all holes. That filter worked correctly with RealSense SDK v2.10.3 (or older versions). ### How To Reproduce This Problem Set the option to filling all holes as follows. But, filter output is not filled all holes. This problem can also be confirmed by setting the [Stereo Module]>[Post-Processing]>[Spatial Filter]>[Holes filling] option to "Unlimited" in RealSense Viewer v2.10.4. ```cpp rs2::spatial_filter spatial_filter; if( spatial_filter.supports( rs2_option::RS2_OPTION_HOLES_FILL ) ){ rs2::option_range option_range = spatial_filter.get_option_range( rs2_option::RS2_OPTION_HOLES_FILL ); spatial_filter.set_option( rs2_option::RS2_OPTION_HOLES_FILL, option_range.max ); } ```

issue_title: Perform post upgrade checks/verification using a Pulp Smash script
issue_body:
Our existing upgrade process relies on a shell [script](https://github.com/pulp/pulp_packaging/blob/master/ci/jobs/scripts/post-upgrade.sh) that calls `pulp-admin` to loop through some expected content [populated](https://github.com/pulp/pulp_packaging/blob/master/ci/jobs/pulp-upgrade.yaml) by a prior task. However, there is no reliable way to use `pulp-admin` to assert that the content populated prior to the upgrade is actually still available and migrated to the newer version.

It would be great to write a Python script which can import Pulp Smash to perform these post upgrade checks and therefore increase our trust in the upgrade jobs.

issue_title: Implementing Travis task to publish docs to static site
issue_body:
Here's the plan to publish documentation smoothly.   Since we need both npm/jsdoc and python/sphinx, we cannot use readthedocs.   Instead we will follow what Mozilla's Fathom project does; they already have the same mix of Pythin and JS documented with Sphinx.  See the heading "Example" in this page....

    https://pypi.python.org/pypi/sphinx-js

So, we write a script which will build the docs in a separate subdirectory, and push them to the gh-pages branch of the athlib repo.  This branch will be "docs and static stuff" only.   It can be served from openath.github.io/athlib/

This can then be run for us by Travis, so a simple push of the code or docs is enough to update the hosted docs.

issue_title: Run model from URDF
issue_body:
Hello,

I have a robot urdf file that I want to use mujoco_py to simulate, so I did the following steps:

1. I add 
```
  <mujoco>
        <compiler meshdir="../meshes/"/>
  </mujoco>
```
  to my URDF file, then use 
`./compile model.urdf model.mjb`
 to convert it to mjb

2. I use the given example as a trySim.py file

```
#!/usr/bin/env python3

from mujoco_py import load_model_from_path, MjSim, MjViewer
from mujoco_py.modder import TextureModder
import os

model = load_model_from_path("model.mjb")
sim = MjSim(model)
viewer = MjViewer(sim)
modder = TextureModder(sim)

t = 0
while True:
    for name in sim.model.geom_names:
        modder.rand_all(name)

    viewer.render()
    t += 1
    if t > 100 and os.getenv('TESTING') is not None:
        break
```

3. I run it by

`sudo python3 trySim.py `

Then the screen blinks once and I get the following error:
```
Creating window glfw
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "trySim.py", line 10, in <module>
    modder = TextureModder(sim)

    tex_id = self.model.mat_texid[mat_id]
TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
```

I confirm that my model.mjb works in mujoco if I run ./simulate and just drag and drop, I also confirm that my trySim.py works for the given humanoid.xml example. So both my model and python code should be fine...

Does anyone know why it happens?
Thank you!

issue_title: Disable salt-minion service on Linux hosts
issue_body:
By default, Watchmaker's installation of the salt packages installs the `salt-minion` service. This service should be stopped and disabled:
* EL6: `chkconfig salt-minion off ; service salt-minion stop`
* EL7: `systemctl stop salt-minion ; systemctl disable salt-minion ; systemctl mask salt-minion`

issue_title: Modernize the style and layout of documentation pages
issue_body:
hotdoc currently uses gtk-doc's css and layout approach, and outputs pages that look like this : 

http://gstreamer.freedesktop.org/data/doc/gstreamer/head/gstreamer/html/GstElement.html

There are two levels of formatting in hotdoc: symbol-level and page-level.

I'll concentrate on the requirements for the page-level design, as I'll assume
symbol-level is pretty well designed already.

Page-level design requirements:

* We want to expose a search function, ideally with autocompletion.
* Individual symbols may be tagged in several ways, for example
  a "since" tag (since: 1.2). We will want to expose an interface
  to filter the page according to these tags, for example if the
  user wants to only see symbols that were defined in the 1.0
  version.
* GObject introspection (GI) allows using C libraries in other
  languages (python and javascript for example). The formatting
  of symbols changes according to the language,
  see https://people.collabora.com/~meh/test_greeter_doc/c/test-greeter.html#test_greeter_do_foo_bar
  vs https://people.collabora.com/~meh/test_greeter_doc/python/test-greeter.html#test_greeter_do_foo_bar
  We want to make it easy for the user to switch from one language to another.
  Note that this is specific to GI, when hotdoc is used
  to document a python library, or a non-GI C library, there is no need for such
  an interface.
* We want the output html to be modular, for example it will be possible
  to output pages without the search interface, without the filtering interface etc.
* There should be both a page-level and website-level navigation interface.
  Page-level navigation will let the user scroll to a given anchor, and 
  website-level navigation should expose the map of the site, for example
  it should be possible from this page :
  https://developer.gnome.org/glib/stable/glib-GVariant.html
  to switch to https://developer.gnome.org/glib/stable/glib-cross-compiling.html
  in one click. We will possibly also want to fold / unfold page-level
  navigation in that website-level navigation view.
* The output pages need to be responsive, and deal well with being displayed
  in a split screen
* Symbols documentation, and standalone markdown pages can be edited,
  the design for this feature is a separate issue, but the possibility
  of editing should be made obvious in the pages.
* The output pages should reserve some optional space to allow for
  basic branding (a logo, ..), and expose copyright and contact
  information, along with hotdoc information (page generated on,
  hotdoc version used, link to the hotdoc project)
* Add more here :)

Note that I am currently working on making the output "css-agnostic", with
the goal of interfacing with twitter-bootstrap easily.

issue_body:

That is, considering to calendars A and B: 

* all events of A are put in B
* if an event is moved in A it is moved in B
* if an event is deleted in A it is deleted in B

* (B may contain events that are not in A)

issue_title: Standalone configurator: Dealing with existing processes that are listening on some, but not all, interfaces
issue_labels: [{'id': 277074172, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20acme', 'name': 'area: acme', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 418985762, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20standalone', 'name': 'area: standalone', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 146954789, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/enhancement', 'name': 'enhancement', 'color': '00ccff', 'default': True}, {'id': 175439992, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/low%20priority', 'name': 'low priority', 'color': 'bfe5bf', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Currently the standalone configurator always tries to listen on 0.0.0.0, which is an error if any other process has the same TCP port bound on _any_ interface (including the loopback interface!).

It would be useful to be able to enumerate interfaces in order to be able to bind only non-loopback interfaces in this particular case. It would also be useful to let the user specify an address to listen on using a command-line option.

There are also future consequences for IPv6 support in how this is handled. (I think the CA currently only performs DVSNI tests over IPv4.)

issue_title: Handle change of channel state on restart gracefully
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Raiden stores serialized state of a channel on exit and uses it to restore state on startup. If the stored state differs from the contract's state, raiden will fail with an exception.
This situation may happen i.e.
- if contract address has changed
- if geth node is connected to a differrent network
- channel is removed from the contract for some reason
- (if user accidentally starts two nodes with the same configuration/account)

## Solution

- store contract addresses on first run inside of configuration db and warn user if the address changes
or
- derive configuration directory/db path from contract addresses . 

## Tasklist
- [ ] Discuss which approach should we use
- [ ] handle state disparity and give the user some meaningful info how to solve the situation

issue_title: Write MVP User Stories
issue_body:
## Requirements
- [ ] A relatively new computer < 5 years old
- [ ] The computer must be connected to the Internet
- [ ] The computer must have Linux or Mac OSX as an operating system
- [ ] A geth node installed and synced
- [ ] An ethereum account with funds available

## Installation
- [ ] A user must be able to access the Raiden website.
- [ ] On the Raiden website the user must be able to navigate to the `Download` section.
- [ ] Under the download section the user must be able to select which Raiden client to download or installation instructions
- [ ] It must be possible to download clients for Linux and read Mac OSX install instructions
- [ ] The website must contain a simple installation guide
- [ ] When the download is done, the user must be able to install the Raiden client with one click/command

## Starting Raiden
- [ ] It must be possible for a user to start the Raiden client with one command
- [ ] The user must be able to access a minimal GUI for interacting with Raiden, once Raiden is started
- [ ] The GUI must present the user with relevant information about the user's raiden channels
- [ ] It must show the current open channels of the user and the status hereof
- [ ] It must show the token networks the user currently participates in
- [ ] If the user does not have any open channels, he/she should be made aware hereof and a button for starting token networks or participating in a token network should be presented
- [ ] The user must be presented to failure modes if geth is not available or no funds are present etc.
- [ ] The user must be presented to a message with instructions for the RPC endpoint/UI

## Adding a token
- [ ] The GUI must allow the user to join a network for an already existing token 
- [ ] It should be possible to create a network for an already existing token using the GUI
- [ ] Tokens held by the account should be highlighted in a token overview
- [ ] There must be a dummy ERC20 token contract that mints new tokens on interaction from the user
- [ ] There must be an echo node for users to interact with
- [ ] The user should not experience a difference between adding a new token network or joining an already existing one

## Opening channels
- [ ] Upon joining a token network the user must specify an amount of tokens to use and he/she should automatically be connected to existing participants of that token network
- [ ] It should be possible to open a direct channel with some specified token and account address 
- [ ] The UI should communicate prior to connecting what the user's capacities in the channels will be (for automatic provisioning)

## Depositing
- [ ] The user must be able to deposit more tokens to a channel than initially deposited (just a documented rpc call)

## Inspecting channels
- [ ] The user must be able to inspect the state of a specific channel
- [ ] the user must be able to see the state of all open channels

## Transfer of a specific token
- [ ] The user must be able to specify the receiving address and the amount he/she wants to transfer

## Closing channels
- [ ] The user should be able to close an open channel
- [ ] The interface must provide a method to shutdown the client and block until it is safe to leave

## Re-entering a raiden network
- [ ] It must be possible for a user to enter a token network after having left it

issue_title: Give run_in_worker_thread a semaphore= argument
issue_body: If you want to limit the number of simultaneous threads, a semaphore is good for that. But right now it doesn't quite work with `run_in_worker_thread(..., cancellable=True)` because you lose track of the thread once it's cancelled. The idea here would be that if cancelled then `run_in_worker_thread` returns immediately... but the semaphore doesn't get released until the thread actual exits.

issue_title: `repr` call after `AppException` instantiation raises `AttributeError`
issue_body:
The custom `str` method of `AppException` just reproduces the base class `str` method. The only thing the custom `repr` does is change the format, but I think we should consider sticking to the baseclass version for improved readability (it matches the built-in format); additionally, it introduces a bug because if `reason` is not set, the `repr` call will fail with an `AttributeError`.
```
In [28]: from parsl.app.errors import ParslError, AppException

In [29]: x = ParslError('baz')

In [30]: y = AppException('baz')

In [31]: x
Out[31]: parsl.app.errors.ParslError('baz')

In [32]: y
Out[32]: parsl.app.errors.AppException('baz')

In [33]: repr(x)
Out[33]: "ParslError('baz',)"

In [34]: repr(y)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-34-2a48cbef3bc0> in <module>()
----> 1 repr(y)

~/ci/parsl/parsl/app/errors.py in __repr__(self)
     40
     41     def __repr__(self):
---> 42         return "{0} Reason:{1}".format(self.__class__, self.reason)
     43
     44     def __str__(self):

AttributeError: 'AppException' object has no attribute 'reason'

In [35]: y.reason = 'baz'

In [36]: repr(y)
Out[36]: "<class 'parsl.app.errors.AppException'> Reason:baz"
```

issue_title: Index operator? Reconsider slice?
issue_body: Currently, indexing is implemented using the `Slice` operator, which is more general. But the conversion is quite disgusting: because slice as implemented only supports slicing in 1D tensors, we have to first reshape a tensor into 1D, convert the indices into a scalar representation, and then shape back to the correct thing. This is a bit awful. Pave the cowpaths.

issue_title: Swap opencv-python for opencv-python-headless
issue_body:
With the release of `opencv-python` [3.3.0.10](https://github.com/skvark/opencv-python/releases/tag/10), new dependencies were added to support Qt. This has made it harder to use `opencv-python` in some server environments were certain libraries might not be available, and/or not possible to install. See https://github.com/skvark/opencv-python/issues/44 for one such example.

In response opencv-python now provides [headless packages](https://pypi.org/project/opencv-python-headless/).

We are using `sentinelhub-py` in a server environment where installing the GUI related dependancies might not be possible, so for now we've just had to pin our opencv-python package to version `3.3.0.9`.

Would it be possible switch sentinelhub-py's requirement on `opencv-python` to `opencv-python-headless`? I've had a look through the code here, and done some testing locally that would seem to suggest the two could be swapped directly.

Interested in any thoughts on this. Thanks.
